sents.en: Of all the changes that have taken place in English-language newspapers during the past quarter-century, perhaps the most far-reaching has been the inexorable decline in the scope and seriousness of their arts coverage.
sents.cn: 在过去二十五年间英文报纸所发生的所有变化中，影响最为深远的或许是其艺术报道在范围和严肃性上不可遏止的衰落。

sents.en: It is difficult to the point of impossibility for the average reader under the age of forty to imagine a time when high-quality arts criticism could be found in most big-city newspapers.
sents.cn: 对于年龄在四十岁以下的普通读者来说，要想象一个曾经能在大多数大城市报纸上读到高水准艺术评论的年代，简直难到了不可能的地步。

sents.en: Yet a considerable number of the most significant collections of criticism published in the 20th century consisted in large part of newspaper reviews.
sents.cn: 然而，20世纪出版的相当一批最为重要的评论集，其内容很大一部分都是由报纸上的评论文章构成的。

sents.en: To read such books today is to marvel at the fact that their learned contents were once deemed suitable for publication in general-circulation dailies.
sents.cn: 今天读到这样的书，会让人惊叹：这些学识渊博的内容竟然一度被认为适合刊登在面向大众发行的日报上。

sents.en: We are even farther removed from the unfocused newspaper reviews published in England between the turn of the 20th century and the eve of World War II, at a time when newsprint was dirt-cheap and stylish arts criticism was considered an ornament to the publications in which it appeared.
sents.cn: 我们与那些在20世纪初到第二次世界大战前夕之间发表于英国的随意发挥的报纸评论，相隔得更为遥远；那个年代新闻纸极为廉价，文笔优雅的艺术评论被视为它所刊载的报刊的一种装点。

sents.en: In those far-off days, it was taken for granted that the critics of major papers would write in detail and at length about the events they covered.
sents.cn: 在那遥远的岁月里，人们想当然地认为，大报的评论家会对他们所报道的事件进行详尽而充分的评述。

sents.en: Theirs was a serious business, and even those reviewers who wore their learning lightly, like George Bernard Shaw and Ernest Newman, could be trusted to know what they were about.
sents.cn: 他们从事的是一项严肃的事业，即便是像萧伯纳和欧内斯特·纽曼那样把学问举重若轻的评论家，人们也尽可信赖他们深谙自己所评论的对象。

sents.en: These men believed in journalism as a calling, and were proud to be published in the daily press.
sents.cn: 这些人把新闻业视为一种神圣的使命，并以自己的文章能在日报上发表而自豪。

sents.en: "So few authors have brains enough or literary gift enough to keep their own end up in journalism," Newman wrote, "that I am tempted to define 'journalism' as 'a term of contempt applied by writers who are not read to writers who are.'"
sents.cn: 纽曼写道：“真正有头脑或有文学天赋、足以在新闻业中独当一面的作者寥寥无几，以至于我都忍不住要把‘新闻业’定义为‘没人读其作品的作家加诸有人读其作品的作家身上的一个轻蔑之词’。”

sents.en: Unfortunately, these critics are virtually forgotten.
sents.cn: 遗憾的是，这些评论家如今几乎都被人遗忘了。

sents.en: Neville Cardus, who wrote for the Manchester Guardian from 1917 until shortly before his death in 1975, is now known solely as a writer of essays on the game of cricket.
sents.cn: 内维尔·卡德斯自1917年起为《曼彻斯特卫报》撰稿，直到1975年去世前不久，如今却仅仅作为一位撰写板球运动随笔的作家而为人所知。

sents.en: During his lifetime, though, he was also one of England's foremost classical-music critics, a stylist so widely admired that his Autobiography (1947) became a best-seller.
sents.cn: 然而在他的有生之年，他还是英国首屈一指的古典音乐评论家之一，其文笔广受赞赏，以至于他的《自传》（1947年）成为了畅销书。

sents.en: He was knighted in 1967, the first music critic to be so honored.
sents.cn: 他于1967年被授予爵士头衔，是首位获此殊荣的音乐评论家。

sents.en: Yet only one of his books is now in print, and his vast body of writings on music is unknown save to specialists.
sents.cn: 然而如今他的著作只有一本仍在印行，他卷帙浩繁的音乐评论除专家之外鲜为人知。

sents.en: Is there any chance that Cardus's criticism will enjoy a revival?
sents.cn: 卡德斯的评论是否还有重获新生的可能？

sents.en: The prospect seems remote.
sents.cn: 这种前景似乎相当渺茫。

sents.en: Journalistic tastes had changed long before his death, and postmodern readers have little use for the richly upholstered Vicwardian prose in which he specialized.
sents.cn: 早在他去世之前，新闻品味就已发生变化，而后现代的读者对他所擅长的那种辞藻华丽的维多利亚–爱德华时代文风几乎毫无兴趣。

sents.en: Moreover, the amateur tradition in music criticism has been in headlong retreat.
sents.cn: 此外，音乐评论中的业余传统也一直在急剧地退却。

sents.en: Over the past decade, thousands of patents have been granted for what are called business methods.
sents.cn: 在过去十年间，成千上万项专利被授予所谓的商业方法。

sents.en: Amazon.com received one for its "one-click" online payment system.
sents.cn: 亚马逊公司就其“一键式”在线支付系统获得了一项专利。

sents.en: Merrill Lynch got legal protection for an asset allocation strategy.
sents.cn: 美林公司为一种资产配置策略获得了法律保护。

sents.en: One inventor patented a technique for lifting a box.
sents.cn: 一位发明者为一种搬箱子的方法申请了专利。

sents.en: Now the nation's top patent court appears completely ready to scale back on business-method patents, which have been controversial ever since they were first authorized 10 years ago.
sents.cn: 如今，美国最高专利法院似乎已完全准备好要收紧商业方法专利，而这类专利自十年前首次获准以来便一直备受争议。

sents.en: In a move that has intellectual-property lawyers abuzz the U.S. court of Appeals for the federal circuit said it would use a particular case to conduct a broad review of business-method patents.
sents.cn: 在一项令知识产权律师们议论纷纷的举措中，美国联邦巡回上诉法院表示，它将借助一起具体案件对商业方法专利展开广泛的审查。

sents.en: In re Bilski, as the case is known, is "a very big deal", says Dennis D. Crouch of the University of Missouri School of law.
sents.cn: 密苏里大学法学院的丹尼斯·D·克劳奇说，这起被称为“比尔斯基案”的案子“是一件大事”。

sents.en: It "has the potential to eliminate an entire class of patents."
sents.cn: 它“有可能使一整类专利不复存在”。

sents.en: Curbs on business-method claims would be a dramatic about-face, because it was the federal circuit itself that introduced such patents with is 1998 decision in the so-called state Street Bank case, approving a patent on a way of pooling mutual-fund assets.
sents.cn: 对商业方法权利主张加以限制将是一次戏剧性的彻底转向，因为正是联邦巡回法院自己，在其1998年对所谓“道富银行案”的判决中引入了此类专利，当时它认可了一项关于汇集共同基金资产之方法的专利。

sents.en: That ruling produced an explosion in business-method patent filings, initially by emerging internet companies trying to stake out exclusive rights to specific types of online transactions.
sents.cn: 那项判决引发了商业方法专利申请的激增，最初是那些新兴的互联网公司，试图为特定类型的在线交易圈定独占权利。

sents.en: Later, move established companies raced to add such patents to their files, if only as a defensive move against rivals that might beat them to the punch.
sents.cn: 后来，那些较为成熟的老牌公司也争相把此类专利添加到自己的专利档案中，哪怕只是为了防范竞争对手抢先一步而采取的一种防御措施。

sents.en: In 2005, IBM noted in a court filing that it had been issued more than 300 business-method patents despite the fact that it questioned the legal basis for granting them.
sents.cn: 2005年，IBM在一份法庭文件中指出，尽管它对授予商业方法专利的法律依据存有疑问，但它本身已获得了300多项此类专利。

sents.en: Similarly, some Wall Street investment films armed themselves with patents for financial products, even as they took positions in court cases opposing the practice.
sents.cn: 与此类似，一些华尔街投资公司也为金融产品申请专利来武装自己，尽管它们在法庭案件中所持的立场是反对这种做法的。

sents.en: The Bilski case involves a claimed patent on a method for hedging risk in the energy market.
sents.cn: 比尔斯基案涉及一项就能源市场风险对冲方法所主张的专利。

sents.en: The Federal circuit issued an unusual order stating that the case would be heard by all 12 of the court's judges, rather than a typical panel of three, and that one issue it wants to evaluate is whether it should "reconsider" its state street Bank ruling.
sents.cn: 联邦巡回法院发布了一道不同寻常的命令，宣布该案将由该院全部12名法官审理，而非通常由三名法官组成的合议庭，并称它想要评判的一个问题是，是否应当“重新审议”其对道富银行案的判决。

sents.en: The Federal Circuit's action comes in the wake of a series of recent decisions by the supreme Court that has narrowed the scope of protections for patent holders.
sents.cn: 联邦巡回法院的这一行动，是在最高法院近来作出一系列判决、缩小了对专利持有人保护范围之后出现的。

sents.en: Last April, for example the justices signaled that too many patents were being upheld for "inventions" that are obvious.
sents.cn: 例如，去年四月，大法官们就发出信号，表示有太多授予“显而易见”之“发明”的专利得到了维持。

sents.en: The judges on the Federal circuit are "reacting to the anti-patent trend at the Supreme Court", says Harold C. Wegner, a patent attorney and professor at George Washington University Law School.
sents.cn: 乔治·华盛顿大学法学院的专利律师兼教授哈罗德·C·韦格纳说，联邦巡回法院的法官们“正在对最高法院的反专利趋势作出回应”。

sents.en: In his book The Tipping Point, Malcolm Gladwell argues that "social epidemics" are driven in large part by the acting of a tiny minority of special individuals, often called influentials, who are unusually informed, persuasive, or well-connected.
sents.cn: 马尔科姆·格拉德威尔在其著作《引爆点》中提出，“社会流行潮”在很大程度上是由极少数特殊人物的行为所推动的，这些人常被称为“关键人物”，他们往往消息异常灵通、极具说服力或人脉极广。

sents.en: The idea is intuitively compelling, but it doesn't explain how ideas actually spread.
sents.cn: 这一观点从直觉上看颇具说服力，但它并未解释观念实际上是如何传播的。

sents.en: The supposed importance of influentials derives from a plausible-sounding but largely untested theory called the "two- step flow of communication": Information flows from the media to the influentials and from them to everyone else.
sents.cn: 关键人物所谓的重要性，源自一种听上去合情合理却在很大程度上未经检验的理论，即“两级传播”：信息从媒体流向关键人物，再由关键人物流向其他所有人。

sents.en: Marketers have embraced the two-step flow because it suggests that if they can just find and influence the influentials, those selected people will do most of the work for them.
sents.cn: 营销人员欣然接受了两级传播理论，因为它意味着：只要他们能找到并影响这些关键人物，那些被选中的人便会替他们完成大部分工作。

sents.en: The theory also seems to explain the sudden and unexpected popularity of certain looks, brands, or neighborhoods.
sents.cn: 该理论似乎还能解释某些造型、品牌或街区何以会突然出人意料地流行起来。

sents.en: In many such cases, a cursory search for causes finds that some small group of people was wearing, promoting, or developing whatever it is before anyone else paid attention.
sents.cn: 在许多此类情形中，对成因稍加探究便会发现，在其他人尚未注意之前，总有某一小群人率先穿着、推广或开创了那种事物。

sents.en: Anecdotal evidence of this kind fits nicely with the idea that only certain special people can drive trends.
sents.cn: 这类轶事性的证据，与“唯有某些特殊人物才能引领潮流”这一观点十分契合。

sents.en: In their recent work, however, some researchers have come up with the finding that influentials have far less impact on social epidemics than is generally supposed.
sents.cn: 然而，一些研究者在其近期的研究中得出了这样的发现：关键人物对社会流行潮的影响远比人们通常所设想的要小得多。

sents.en: In fact, they don't seem to be required of all.
sents.cn: 事实上，他们似乎根本就并非必不可少。

sents.en: The researchers' argument stems from a simple observing about social influence: With the exception of a few celebrities like Oprah Winfrey—whose outsize presence is primarily a function of media, not interpersonal, influence—even the most influential members of a population simply don't interact with that many others.
sents.cn: 研究者们的论点源于一个关于社会影响的简单观察：除了奥普拉·温弗瑞这样的少数名人——其超凡的影响力主要源自媒体影响而非人际影响——之外，即便是某一群体中最具影响力的成员，与他人的交往其实也并不那么广泛。

sents.en: Yet it is precisely these non-celebrity influentials who, according to the two-step-flow theory, are supposed to drive social epidemics, by influencing their friends and colleagues directly.
sents.cn: 然而，按照两级传播理论，恰恰正是这些并非名人的关键人物，被认为应当通过直接影响其亲友和同事来推动社会流行潮。

sents.en: For a social epidemic to occur, however, each person so affected, must then influence his or her own acquaintances, who must in turn influence theirs, and so on; and just how many others pay attention to each of these people has little to do with the initial influential.
sents.cn: 然而，要使一场社会流行潮得以发生，每一个如此受到影响的人就必须进而去影响他或她自己的熟人，这些熟人又必须转而去影响他们的熟人，如此层层相传；而究竟有多少人会关注这些人当中的每一个，与那个最初的关键人物却几乎毫无关系。

sents.en: If people in the network just two degrees removed from the initial influential prove resistant, for example, the cascade of change won't propagate very far or affect many people.
sents.cn: 例如，倘若网络中与最初那位关键人物仅隔两层的人表现出抗拒，那么这场变化的连锁反应便不会传播得很远，也不会影响到很多人。

sents.en: Building on the basic truth about interpersonal influence, the researchers studied the dynamics of social influence by conducting thousands of computer simulations of populations, manipulating a number of variables relating to people's ability to influence others and their tendency to be influenced.
sents.cn: 基于这一关于人际影响的基本事实，研究者们通过对人群进行数千次的计算机模拟来研究社会影响的动态规律，其间对若干变量加以调控，这些变量涉及人们影响他人的能力以及他们易受他人影响的倾向。

sents.en: They found that the principal requirement for what is called "global cascades"—the widespread propagation of influence through networks—is the presence not of a few influentials but, rather, of a critical mass of easily influenced people.
sents.cn: 他们发现，所谓“全局连锁反应”——即影响通过网络的广泛传播——所需的首要条件，并非是少数几个关键人物的存在，而是要有数量达到临界规模的易受影响之人。

sents.en: Bankers have been blaming themselves for their troubles in public.
sents.cn: 银行家们一直在公开场合为自己的困境责备自己。

sents.en: Behind the scenes, they have been taking aim at someone else: the accounting standard-setters.
sents.cn: 而在幕后，他们却把矛头对准了另一些人：会计准则的制定者。

sents.en: Their rules, moan the banks, have forced them to report enormous losses, and it's just not fair.
sents.cn: 银行家们抱怨说，这些准则迫使他们报出巨额亏损，这实在不公平。

sents.en: These rules say they must value some assets at the price a third party would pay, not the price managers and regulators would like them to fetch.
sents.cn: 这些准则规定，他们必须按第三方愿意支付的价格、而非按管理者和监管者希望其卖出的价格来对某些资产进行估值。

sents.en: Unfortunately, banks' lobbying now seems to be working.
sents.cn: 遗憾的是，银行的游说如今似乎正在奏效。

sents.en: The details may be unknowable, but the independence of standard-setters, essential to the proper functioning of capital markets, is being compromised.
sents.cn: 其中的细节也许无从知晓，但对资本市场正常运转至关重要的准则制定者的独立性，正在受到损害。

sents.en: And, unless banks carry toxic assets at prices that attract buyers, reviving the banking system will be difficult.
sents.cn: 而且，除非银行以能够吸引买家的价格持有不良资产，否则要使银行体系恢复生机将十分困难。

sents.en: After a bruising encounter with Congress, America's Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) rushed through rule changes.
sents.cn: 在与国会一番激烈交锋之后，美国财务会计准则委员会（FASB）匆忙通过了准则的修改。

sents.en: These gave banks more freedom to use models to value illiquid assets and more flexibility in recognizing losses on long-term assets in their income statement.
sents.cn: 这些修改赋予了银行更大的自由，可以运用模型来对流动性差的资产进行估值，并在损益表中确认长期资产损失时拥有更大的灵活性。

sents.en: Bob Herz, the FASB's chairman, cried out against those who "question our motives."
sents.cn: FASB主席鲍勃·赫兹对那些“质疑我们动机”的人大声疾呼予以反驳。

sents.en: Yet bank shares rose and the changes enhance what one lobby group politely calls "the use of judgment by management."
sents.cn: 然而银行股价随之上涨，而这些修改增强了某一游说团体委婉地称之为“管理层运用判断”的余地。

sents.en: European ministers instantly demanded that the International Accounting Standards Board (IASB) do likewise.
sents.cn: 欧洲各国部长们立即要求国际会计准则理事会（IASB）也照此办理。

sents.en: The IASB says it does not want to act without overall planning, but the pressure to fold when it completes it reconstruction of rules later this year is strong.
sents.cn: IASB表示它不愿在缺乏整体规划的情况下贸然行动，但当它在今年晚些时候完成对准则的重构时，要其屈服让步的压力十分强大。

sents.en: Charlie McCreevy, a European commissioner, warned the IASB that it did "not live in a political vacuum" but "in the real word" and that Europe could yet develop different rules.
sents.cn: 欧盟委员查理·麦克里维警告IASB说，它并“不是生活在政治真空之中”，而是“生活在现实世界里”，并称欧洲终究有可能制定出另一套不同的准则。

sents.en: It was banks that were on the wrong planet, with accounts that vastly overvalued assets.
sents.cn: 当初恰恰是银行自己置身于另一个星球，其账目把资产估值高得离谱。

sents.en: Today they argue that market prices overstate losses, because they largely reflect the temporary illiquidity of markets, not the likely extent of bad debts.
sents.cn: 如今他们却辩称，市场价格夸大了亏损，因为这些价格在很大程度上反映的是市场暂时的流动性匮乏，而非坏账可能达到的规模。

sents.en: The truth will not be known for years.
sents.cn: 真相在未来若干年内都将无从知晓。

sents.en: But bank's shares trade below their book value, suggesting that investors are skeptical.
sents.cn: 但银行股票的交易价格已低于其账面价值，这表明投资者对此心存怀疑。

sents.en: And dead markets partly reflect the paralysis of banks which will not sell assets for fear of booking losses, yet are reluctant to buy all those supposed bargains.
sents.cn: 而毫无生气的市场在一定程度上反映出银行的瘫痪状态：它们因为害怕入账亏损而不愿出售资产，却又不情愿去买进所有那些所谓的便宜货。

sents.en: To get the system working again, losses must be recognized and dealt with.
sents.cn: 要让这一体系重新运转起来，亏损就必须得到确认并加以处理。

sents.en: America's new plan to buy up toxic assets will not work unless banks mark assets to levels which buyers find attractive.
sents.cn: 美国收购不良资产的新计划将不会奏效，除非银行把资产估值调整到买家觉得有吸引力的水平。

sents.en: Successful markets require independent and even combative standard-setters.
sents.cn: 成功的市场需要独立、乃至敢于据理力争的准则制定者。

sents.en: The FASB and IASB have been exactly that, cleaning up rules on stock options and pensions, for example, against hostility from special interests.
sents.cn: FASB和IASB一向正是如此，例如，它们曾不顾特殊利益集团的敌意，整顿了关于股票期权和养老金的准则。

sents.en: But by giving in to critics now they are inviting pressure to make more concessions.
sents.cn: 但如今对批评者作出让步，无异于招致更多要它们妥协退让的压力。

sents.en: The decision of the New York Philharmonic to hire Alan Gilbert as its next music director has been the talk of the classical-music world ever since the sudden announcement of his appointment in 2009.
sents.cn: 自2009年纽约爱乐乐团突然宣布聘任艾伦·吉尔伯特担任下一任音乐总监以来，这一决定便一直是古典音乐界热议的话题。

sents.en: For the most part, the response has been favorable, to say the least.
sents.cn: 在大多数情况下，至少可以说，各方的反应是积极的。

sents.en: "Hooray! At last!" wrote Anthony Tommasini, a sober-sided classical-music critic.
sents.cn: “好哇！终于等到了！”态度严肃的古典音乐评论家安东尼·托马西尼写道。

sents.en: One of the reasons why the appointment came as such a surprise, however, is that Gilbert is comparatively little known.
sents.cn: 然而，这一任命之所以如此出人意料，原因之一在于吉尔伯特相对而言名气不大。

sents.en: Even Tommasini, who had advocated Gilbert's appointment in the Times, calls him "an unpretentious musician with no air of the formidable conductor about him."
sents.cn: 就连曾在《纽约时报》上力荐吉尔伯特出任该职的托马西尼，也称他是“一位毫不矫饰的音乐家，身上没有那种令人敬畏的指挥家的派头”。

sents.en: As a description of the next music director of an orchestra that has hitherto been led by musicians like Gustav Mahler and Pierre Boulez, that seems likely to have struck at least some Times readers as faint praise.
sents.cn: 作为对这样一支乐团下一任音乐总监的描述——该乐团此前曾由古斯塔夫·马勒和皮埃尔·布列兹这样的音乐家执掌——这番话恐怕会让至少一部分《纽约时报》的读者觉得不过是不痛不痒的赞许。

sents.en: For my part, I have no idea whether Gilbert is a great conductor or even a good one.
sents.cn: 就我个人而言，我不知道吉尔伯特是不是一位伟大的指挥家，甚至不知道他算不算一位优秀的指挥家。

sents.en: To be sure, he performs an impressive variety of interesting compositions, but it is not necessary for me to visit Avery Fisher Hall, or anywhere else, to hear interesting orchestral music.
sents.cn: 诚然，他演绎了种类繁多、令人印象深刻的有趣作品，但要欣赏有趣的管弦乐，我并不一定非得去艾弗里·费舍尔音乐厅，或是其他任何地方。

sents.en: All I have to do is to go to my CD shelf, or boot up my computer and download still more recorded music from iTunes.
sents.cn: 我所要做的，无非是走到自己的CD架前，或者打开电脑，从iTunes上下载更多的录制音乐。

sents.en: Devoted concertgoers who reply that recordings are no substitute for live performance are missing the point.
sents.cn: 那些虔诚的音乐会常客辩称录音无法取代现场演奏，他们其实没有抓住要点。

sents.en: For the time, attention, and money of the art-loving public, classical instrumentalists must compete not only with opera houses, dance troupes, theater companies, and museums, but also with the recorded performances of the great classical musicians of the 20thcentury.
sents.cn: 为了争夺热爱艺术的公众的时间、注意力和金钱，古典器乐演奏家不仅要与歌剧院、舞蹈团、剧团和博物馆竞争，还要与20世纪那些伟大古典音乐家的录音作品竞争。

sents.en: These recordings are cheap, available everywhere, and very often much higher in artistic quality than today's live performances; moreover, they can be "consumed" at a time and place of the listener's choosing.
sents.cn: 这些录音价格低廉、随处可得，而且其艺术质量往往远高于当今的现场演奏；此外，听众还可以在自己选定的时间和地点“享用”它们。

sents.en: The widespread availability of such recordings has thus brought about a crisis in the institution of the traditional classical concert.
sents.cn: 因此，此类录音的广泛普及给传统古典音乐会这一体制带来了危机。

sents.en: One possible response is for classical performers to program attractive new music that is not yet available on record.
sents.cn: 一种可能的应对之策，是古典音乐演奏家上演那些尚无录音、富有吸引力的新作品。

sents.en: Gilbert's own interest in new music has been widely noted: Alex Ross, a classical-music critic, has described him as a man who is capable of turning the Philharmonic into "a markedly different, more vibrant organization."
sents.cn: 吉尔伯特本人对新音乐的兴趣已广受关注：古典音乐评论家亚历克斯·罗斯曾形容他是一个能够把爱乐乐团变成“一个面貌迥异、更具活力的团体”的人。

sents.en: But what will be the nature of that difference?
sents.cn: 但这种变化的实质又会是什么呢？

sents.en: Merely expanding the orchestra's repertoire will not be enough.
sents.cn: 仅仅扩充乐团的曲目是远远不够的。

sents.en: If Gilbert and the Philharmonic are to succeed, they must first change the relationship between America's oldest orchestra and the new audience it hopes to attract.
sents.cn: 如果吉尔伯特和爱乐乐团想要取得成功，他们首先必须改变这支美国历史最悠久的乐团与其希望吸引的新听众之间的关系。

sents.en: When Liam McGee departed as president of Bank of America in August, his explanation was surprisingly straight up.
sents.cn: 今年8月，利亚姆·麦基卸任美国银行总裁时，他给出的解释坦率得令人意外。

sents.en: Rather than cloaking his exit in the usual vague excuses, he came right out and said he was leaving "to pursue my goal of running a company."
sents.cn: 他没有像通常那样用含糊其辞的借口来掩饰自己的离职，而是直截了当地表示，他离开是“为了追求自己执掌一家公司的目标”。

sents.en: Broadcasting his ambition was "very much my decision," McGee says.
sents.cn: 麦基说，公开宣示自己的抱负“完全是我自己的决定”。

sents.en: Within two weeks, he was talking for the first time with the board of Hartford Financial Services Group, which named him CEO and chairman on September 29.
sents.cn: 不到两周，他便首次与哈特福德金融服务集团的董事会进行了接洽，该集团于9月29日任命他为首席执行官兼董事长。

sents.en: McGee says leaving without a position lined up gave him time to reflect on what kind of company he wanted to run.
sents.cn: 麦基说，没有事先安排好职位就离职，给了他时间去思考自己想要执掌一家什么样的公司。

sents.en: It also sent a clear message to the outside world about his aspirations.
sents.cn: 这也向外界清楚地传递出他的志向。

sents.en: And McGee isn't alone.
sents.cn: 麦基并非个例。

sents.en: In recent weeks the No. 2 executives at Avon and American Express quit with the explanation that they were looking for a CEO post.
sents.cn: 最近几周，雅芳和美国运通的二号高管相继辞职，理由都是他们在寻求首席执行官的职位。

sents.en: As boards scrutinize succession plans in response to shareholder pressure, executives who don't get the nod also may wish to move on.
sents.cn: 随着各董事会迫于股东压力而审视接班人计划，那些未获青睐的高管或许也会萌生去意。

sents.en: A turbulent business environment also has senior managers cautious of letting vague pronouncements cloud their reputations.
sents.cn: 动荡的商业环境也使高级管理人员谨慎起来，唯恐含糊其辞的说辞会损害自己的声誉。

sents.en: As the first signs of recovery begin to take hold, deputy chiefs may be more willing to make the jump without a net.
sents.cn: 随着经济复苏的初步迹象开始显现，副手们或许会更愿意在没有退路的情况下放手一搏。

sents.en: In the third quarter, CEO turnover was down 23% from a year ago as nervous boards stuck with the leaders they had, according to Liberum Research.
sents.cn: 据Liberum研究公司称，第三季度首席执行官的更替率比一年前下降了23%，因为忧心忡忡的董事会都留住了原有的领导者。

sents.en: As the economy picks up, opportunities will abound for aspiring leaders.
sents.cn: 随着经济回暖，胸怀抱负的领导者将会有大量机会。

sents.en: The decision to quit a senior position to look for a better one is unconventional.
sents.cn: 辞去高管职位去谋求一个更好职位的决定，是不合常规的。

sents.en: For years executives and headhunters have adhered to the rule that the most attractive CEO candidates are the ones who must be poached.
sents.cn: 多年来，高管和猎头们一直奉行这样一条准则：最具吸引力的首席执行官人选，是那些必须被挖角才能得到的人。

sents.en: Says Korn/Ferry senior partner Dennis Carey: "I can't think of a single search I've done where a board has not instructed me to look at sitting CEOs first."
sents.cn: 光辉国际公司高级合伙人丹尼斯·凯里说：“我所做过的每一次猎聘中，没有哪一次董事会不是指示我先去物色在任的首席执行官。”

sents.en: Those who jumped without a job haven't always landed in top positions quickly.
sents.cn: 那些没有找好工作就贸然离职的人，并不总能很快谋得高位。

sents.en: Ellen Marram quit as chief of Tropicana a decade ago, saying she wanted to be a CEO.
sents.cn: 十年前，埃伦·马拉姆辞去纯果乐公司主管一职，称自己想成为一名首席执行官。

sents.en: It was a year before she became head of a tiny Internet-based commodities exchange.
sents.cn: 时隔一年，她才当上一家小型网络商品交易所的负责人。

sents.en: Robert Willumstad left Citigroup in 2005 with ambitions to be a CEO.
sents.cn: 罗伯特·威伦斯塔德于2005年离开花旗集团，志在成为一名首席执行官。

sents.en: He finally took that post at a major financial institution three years later.
sents.cn: 三年之后，他才在一家大型金融机构谋得了那个职位。

sents.en: Many recruiters say the old disgrace is fading for top performers.
sents.cn: 许多招聘人员表示，对业绩出众者而言，过去那种难堪的看法正在淡化。

sents.en: The financial crisis has made it more acceptable to be between jobs or to leave a bad one.
sents.cn: 金融危机使得处于待业状态或离开一份糟糕的工作变得更容易为人接受。

sents.en: "The traditional rule was it's safer to stay where you are, but that's been fundamentally inverted," says one headhunter.
sents.cn: 一位猎头说：“传统准则认为，待在原地更为稳妥，但这一观念已被彻底颠覆。”

sents.en: "The people who've been hurt the worst are those who've stayed too long."
sents.cn: “受伤害最深的，恰恰是那些待得太久的人。”

sents.en: The rough guide to marketing success used to be that you got what you paid for.
sents.cn: 过去，衡量营销成败的粗略法则是：一分价钱一分货。

sents.en: No longer.
sents.cn: 如今情况已非如此。

sents.en: While traditional "paid" media—such as television commercials and print advertisements – still play a major role, companies today can exploit many alternative forms of media.
sents.cn: 尽管诸如电视广告和平面广告之类的传统“付费”媒体仍然发挥着重要作用，但今天的企业可以利用许多其他形式的媒体。

sents.en: Consumers passionate about a product may create "earned" media by willingly promoting it to friends, and a company may leverage "owned" media by sending e-mail alerts about products and sales to customers registered with its Web site.
sents.cn: 热衷于某种产品的消费者可能会自愿向朋友推荐该产品，从而创造出“赢得”媒体；而企业则可以通过向在其网站上注册的顾客发送有关产品和促销的电子邮件提醒，来利用“自有”媒体。

sents.en: The way consumers now approach the process of making purchase decisions means that marketing's impact stems from a broad range of factors beyond conventional paid media.
sents.cn: 消费者如今做出购买决策的方式意味着，营销的影响力源自传统付费媒体之外的诸多因素。

sents.en: Paid and owned media are controlled by marketers promoting their own products.
sents.cn: 付费媒体和自有媒体由推广自身产品的营销者所掌控。

sents.en: For earned media, such marketers act as the initiator for users' responses.
sents.cn: 至于赢得媒体，这类营销者扮演的是引发用户反应的发起者角色。

sents.en: But in some cases, one marketer's owned media become another marketer's paid media – for instance, when an e-commerce retailer sells ad space on its Web site.
sents.cn: 但在某些情况下，一个营销者的自有媒体会变成另一个营销者的付费媒体——比如，当一家电子商务零售商在其网站上出售广告位时便是如此。

sents.en: We define such sold media as owned media whose traffic is so strong that other organizations place their content or e-commerce engines within that environment.
sents.cn: 我们把这类售出媒体定义为这样一种自有媒体：其流量如此之大，以至于其他机构愿意把自己的内容或电子商务引擎置于该环境之中。

sents.en: This trend, which we believe is still in its infancy, effectively began with retailers and travel providers such as airlines and hotels and will no doubt go further.
sents.cn: 我们认为这一趋势仍处于萌芽阶段，它实际上发端于零售商以及航空公司、酒店之类的旅游服务提供商，并且无疑还将进一步发展。

sents.en: Johnson & Johnson, for example, has created BabyCenter, a stand-alone media property that promotes complementary and even competitive products.
sents.cn: 例如，强生公司创建了BabyCenter（育儿中心），这是一个独立的媒体平台，既推广互补产品，甚至还推广竞争产品。

sents.en: Besides generating income, the presence of other marketers makes the site seem objective, gives companies opportunities to learn valuable information about the appeal of other companies' marketing, and may help expand user traffic for all companies concerned.
sents.cn: 除了创造收益之外，其他营销者的入驻还使该网站显得更加客观，让各家公司有机会了解关于其他公司营销吸引力的宝贵信息，并且可能有助于扩大所有相关公司的用户流量。

sents.en: The same dramatic technological changes that have provided marketers with more (and more diverse) communications choices have also increased the risk that passionate consumers will voice their opinions in quicker, more visible, and much more damaging ways.
sents.cn: 正是这些为营销者提供了更多（且更多样化）传播选择的巨大技术变革，同时也增大了这样一种风险：满腔热情的消费者会以更快、更显眼、破坏性更大的方式表达自己的看法。

sents.en: Such hijacked media are the opposite of earned media: an asset or campaign becomes hostage to consumers, other stakeholders, or activists who make negative allegations about a brand or product.
sents.cn: 这类被劫持的媒体与赢得媒体恰恰相反：一项资产或一场营销活动沦为消费者、其他利益相关者或那些对某品牌或产品发出负面指控的活动人士的人质。

sents.en: Members of social networks, for instance, are learning that they can hijack media to apply pressure on the businesses that originally created them.
sents.cn: 例如，社交网络的成员们正逐渐意识到，他们可以劫持媒体，以此向最初创建这些媒体的企业施压。

sents.en: If that happens, passionate consumers would try to persuade others to boycott products, putting the reputation of the target company at risk.
sents.cn: 一旦这种情况发生，满腔热情的消费者就会设法说服他人抵制相关产品，从而使目标公司的声誉面临风险。

sents.en: In such a case, the company's response may not be sufficiently quick or thoughtful, and the learning curve has been steep.
sents.cn: 在这种情况下，公司的反应可能不够迅速或不够周全，而这其中的学习过程一直十分艰难。

sents.en: Toyota Motor, for example, alleviated some of the damage from its recall crisis earlier this year with a relatively quick and well-orchestrated social-media response campaign, which included efforts to engage with consumers directly on sites such as Twitter and the social-news site Digg.
sents.cn: 例如，丰田汽车公司在今年早些时候，凭借一场反应相对迅速、组织有序的社交媒体应对行动，减轻了其召回危机所造成的部分损害；这场行动包括在Twitter以及社交新闻网站Digg等平台上直接与消费者互动的种种努力。

sents.en: It's no surprise that Jennifer Senior's insightful, provocative magazine cover story, "I Love My Children, I Hate My Life," is arousing much chatter – nothing gets people talking like the suggestion that child rearing is anything less than a completely fulfilling, life-enriching experience.
sents.cn: 詹妮弗·西尼尔那篇见解深刻、引人争议的杂志封面文章《我爱我的孩子，我恨我的生活》引发了大量议论，这毫不令人意外——没有什么比这样一种暗示更能引发人们的热议了：养育孩子算不上是一种全然令人满足、让生活更加丰盈的体验。

sents.en: Rather than concluding that children make parents either happy or miserable, Senior suggests we need to redefine happiness: instead of thinking of it as something that can be measured by moment-to-moment joy, we should consider being happy as a past-tense condition.
sents.cn: 西尼尔并未断言孩子会让父母或快乐或痛苦，而是提出我们需要重新定义幸福：与其把幸福看作一种可以用每时每刻的喜悦来衡量的东西，不如把幸福视为一种过去时态的状态。

sents.en: Even though the day-to-day experience of raising kids can be soul-crushingly hard, Senior writes that "the very things that in the moment dampen our moods can later be sources of intense gratification and delight."
sents.cn: 西尼尔写道，尽管养育孩子的日常经历可能艰辛得令人身心俱疲，但“那些在当下让我们情绪低落的事情，日后却可能成为强烈满足与喜悦的源泉”。

sents.en: The magazine cover showing an attractive mother holding a cute baby is hardly the only Madonna-and-child image on newsstands this week.
sents.cn: 那期杂志封面上，一位漂亮的母亲抱着一个可爱的婴儿，而这绝非本周报摊上唯一的“圣母与圣婴”式画面。

sents.en: There are also stories about newly adoptive – and newly single – mom Sandra Bullock, as well as the usual "Jennifer Aniston is pregnant" news.
sents.cn: 此外，还有关于新近收养孩子、同时又新近成为单身母亲的桑德拉·布洛克的报道，以及那条老生常谈的“詹妮弗·安妮斯顿怀孕了”的新闻。

sents.en: Practically every week features at least one celebrity mom, or mom-to-be, smiling on the newsstands.
sents.cn: 几乎每一周，报摊上都至少会出现一位面带微笑的明星妈妈或准妈妈。

sents.en: In a society that so persistently celebrates procreation, is it any wonder that admitting you regret having children is equivalent to admitting you support kitten-killing?
sents.cn: 在一个如此执着地颂扬生育的社会里，承认自己后悔生了孩子无异于承认自己支持虐杀小猫，这又有什么可奇怪的呢？

sents.en: It doesn't seem quite fair, then, to compare the regrets of parents to the regrets of the childless.
sents.cn: 因此，把为人父母者的悔恨与没有孩子者的悔恨相提并论，似乎并不十分公平。

sents.en: Unhappy parents rarely are provoked to wonder if they shouldn't have had kids, but unhappy childless folks are bothered with the message that children are the single most important thing in the world:obviously their misery must be a direct result of the gaping baby-size holes in their lives.
sents.cn: 不快乐的父母很少会被激得去想自己当初是不是不该要孩子，而不快乐的无子女者却饱受这样一种信息的困扰：孩子是这世上唯一最重要的东西；显然，他们的痛苦必定是其生活中那个婴儿般大小的空洞所直接造成的。

sents.en: Of course, the image of parenthood that celebrity magazines like Us Weekly and People present is hugely unrealistic, especially when the parents are single mothers like Bullock.
sents.cn: 当然，《美国周刊》和《人物》这类明星杂志所呈现的为人父母的形象是极不真实的，尤其是当那些父母像布洛克一样是单身母亲时。

sents.en: According to several studies concluding that parents are less happy than childless couples, single parents are the least happy of all.
sents.cn: 若干研究得出结论认为，为人父母者不如没有孩子的夫妇快乐，而单身父母则是所有人中最不快乐的。

sents.en: No shock there, considering how much work it is to raise a kid without a partner to lean on; yet to hear Sandra and Britney tell it, raising a kid on their "own" (read: with round-the-clock help) is a piece of cake.
sents.cn: 考虑到在没有伴侣可以依靠的情况下独自养育一个孩子是何等繁重的工作，这一点并不令人意外；然而，照桑德拉和布兰妮的说法，“独自”养育孩子（言下之意是：有全天候的帮手）简直是小菜一碟。

sents.en: It's hard to imagine that many people are dumb enough to want children just because Reese and Angelina make it look so glamorous: most adults understand that a baby is not a haircut.
sents.cn: 很难想象会有许多人愚蠢到仅仅因为里斯和安吉丽娜把养孩子演绎得如此光鲜迷人，就想要孩子：大多数成年人都明白，生孩子可不像理个发那么简单。

sents.en: But it's interesting to wonder if the images we see every week of stress-free, happiness-enhancing parenthood aren't in some small, subconscious way contributing to our own dissatisfactions with the actual experience, in the same way that a small part of us hoped getting "the Rachel" might make us look just a little bit like Jennifer Aniston.
sents.cn: 但有趣的是，不妨设想一下：我们每周看到的那些无压力、能增添幸福感的为人父母的画面，是否正以某种微小而潜意识的方式，加剧着我们自己对养育孩子这一真实体验的不满——这就如同我们内心深处曾有一小部分希望，剪个“瑞秋头”或许能让自己看起来稍稍有几分像詹妮弗·安妮斯顿。

sents.en: Come on — Everybody's doing it.
sents.cn: 来吧——大家都在这么做。

sents.en: That whispered message, half invitation and half forcing, is what most of us think of when we hear the words peer pressure.
sents.cn: 这句半是邀请、半是强迫的悄悄话，正是我们大多数人一听到“同伴压力”这个词时所想到的情景。

sents.en: It usually leads to no good — drinking, drugs and casual sex.
sents.cn: 它通常不会带来什么好结果——酗酒、吸毒和随意的性行为。

sents.en: But in her new book Join the Club, Tina Rosenberg contends that peer pressure can also be a positive force through what she calls the social cure, in which organizations and officials use the power of group dynamics to help individuals improve their lives and possibly the world.
sents.cn: 然而，蒂娜·罗森伯格在其新书《加入俱乐部》中提出，通过她所称的“社会疗法”，同伴压力也可以成为一种积极的力量；在这种疗法中，组织和官员利用群体动力的力量，帮助个人改善自己的生活，乃至改善整个世界。

sents.en: Rosenberg, the recipient of a Pulitzer Prize, offers a host of examples of the social cure in action: In South Carolina, a state-sponsored antismoking program called Rage Against the Haze sets out to make cigarettes uncool.
sents.cn: 身为普利策奖得主的罗森伯格列举了大量社会疗法付诸实践的事例：在南卡罗来纳州，一项名为“向烟雾宣战”的州政府资助的反吸烟项目，着手让香烟变得不再时髦。

sents.en: In South Africa, an HIV-prevention initiative known as loveLife recruits young people to promote safe sex among their peers.
sents.cn: 在南非，一项名为“热爱生命”的预防艾滋病行动招募年轻人，在同龄人中推广安全的性行为。

sents.en: The idea seems promising, and Rosenberg is a perceptive observer.
sents.cn: 这一构想看似前景可期，而罗森伯格也是一位富有洞察力的观察者。

sents.en: Her critique of the lameness of many public-health campaigns is spot-on: they fail to mobilize peer pressure for healthy habits, and they demonstrate a seriously flawed understanding of psychology.
sents.cn: 她对许多公共卫生宣传活动之拙劣的批评一针见血：这些活动未能调动同伴压力去培养健康习惯，而且暴露出它们对心理学有着严重错误的理解。

sents.en: "Dare to be different, please don't smoke!" pleads one billboard campaign aimed at reducing smoking among teenagers — teenagers, who desire nothing more than fitting in.
sents.cn: “敢于与众不同，请勿吸烟！”一项旨在减少青少年吸烟的广告牌宣传如此恳求——可青少年最渴望的莫过于融入群体。

sents.en: Rosenberg argues convincingly that public-health advocates ought to take a page from advertisers, so skilled at applying peer pressure.
sents.cn: 罗森伯格令人信服地指出，公共卫生倡导者理应借鉴广告商的做法，因为后者极善于运用同伴压力。

sents.en: But on the general effectiveness of the social cure, Rosenberg is less persuasive.
sents.cn: 但在论及社会疗法的总体成效时，罗森伯格的论述就不那么有说服力了。

sents.en: Join the Club is filled with too much irrelevant detail and not enough exploration of the social and biological factors that make peer pressure so powerful.
sents.cn: 《加入俱乐部》一书充斥着过多无关紧要的细节，对那些使同伴压力如此强大的社会因素和生物因素却探讨不足。

sents.en: The most glaring flaw of the social cure as it's presented here is that it doesn't work very well for very long.
sents.cn: 按照书中的呈现方式，社会疗法最明显的缺陷在于，它既不太奏效，也维持不了多久。

sents.en: Rage Against the Haze failed once state funding was cut.
sents.cn: 一旦州政府的拨款被削减，“向烟雾宣战”项目便归于失败。

sents.en: Evidence that the loveLife program produces lasting changes is limited and mixed.
sents.cn: 能证明“热爱生命”项目带来持久改变的证据既有限又相互矛盾。

sents.en: There's no doubt that our peer groups exert enormous influence on our behavior.
sents.cn: 毫无疑问，我们所处的同伴群体对我们的行为施加着巨大的影响。

sents.en: An emerging body of research shows that positive health habits — as well as negative ones — spread through networks of friends via social communication.
sents.cn: 一批新近涌现的研究表明，健康的生活习惯——以及不健康的习惯——会通过社会交往在朋友的人际网络中传播开来。

sents.en: This is a subtle form of peer pressure: we unconsciously imitate the behavior we see every day.
sents.cn: 这是一种微妙的同伴压力：我们会不知不觉地模仿自己每天所看到的行为。

sents.en: Far less certain, however, is how successfully experts and bureaucrats can select our peer groups and steer their activities in virtuous directions.
sents.cn: 然而，远不那么确定的是，专家和官僚究竟能多么成功地为我们挑选同伴群体，并把他们的活动引向良性的方向。

sents.en: It's like the teacher who breaks up the troublemakers in the back row by pairing them with better-behaved classmates.
sents.cn: 这就好比一位老师，为了拆散后排那些捣乱的学生，把他们与表现较好的同学编排在一起。

sents.en: The tactic never really works.
sents.cn: 这种策略其实从来都不真正奏效。

sents.en: And that's the problem with a social cure engineered from the outside: in the real world, as in school, we insist on choosing our own friends.
sents.cn: 而这正是由外部一手设计的社会疗法所存在的问题：在现实世界中，正如在学校里一样，我们坚持要自己选择朋友。

sents.en: A deal is a deal — except, apparently, when Entergy is involved.
sents.cn: 说话算数本应天经地义——但显然，一旦牵涉到安特吉公司，情况就另当别论了。

sents.en: The company, a major energy supplier in New England, provoked justified outrage in Vermont last week when it announced it was reneging on a longstanding commitment to abide by the strict nuclear regulations.
sents.cn: 上周，这家新英格兰地区的主要能源供应商在佛蒙特州引发了正当的公愤，因为它宣布将背弃自己长期以来作出的、承诺遵守严格核管理规定的承诺。

sents.en: Instead, the company has done precisely what it had long promised it would not: challenge the constitutionality of Vermont's rules in the federal court, as part of a desperate effort to keep its Vermont Yankee nuclear power plant running.
sents.cn: 相反，这家公司恰恰做了它长期以来一直承诺绝不会做的事：在联邦法院挑战佛蒙特州相关规定的合宪性，作为其孤注一掷地维持其佛蒙特扬基核电站继续运营的努力的一部分。

sents.en: It's a stunning move.
sents.cn: 这是令人震惊的一步。

sents.en: The conflict has been surfacing since 2002, when the corporation bought Vermont's only nuclear power plant, an aging reactor in Vernon.
sents.cn: 这场冲突自2002年起便已逐渐浮出水面，当时该公司收购了佛蒙特州唯一的核电站——位于弗农、一座日渐老化的反应堆。

sents.en: As a condition of receiving state approval for the sale, the company agreed to seek permission from state regulators to operate past 2012.
sents.cn: 作为获得州政府批准此项收购的一项条件，该公司同意，若要在2012年以后继续运营，须向州监管机构申请许可。

sents.en: In 2006, the state went a step further, requiring that any extension of the plant's license be subject to Vermont legislature's approval.
sents.cn: 2006年，该州又进一步规定，该电站运营许可的任何延期都必须经佛蒙特州议会批准。

sents.en: Then, too, the company went along.
sents.cn: 当时，该公司也照办了。

sents.en: Either Entergy never really intended to live by those commitments, or it simply didn't foresee what would happen next.
sents.cn: 要么是安特吉公司从一开始就根本无意信守这些承诺，要么就是它压根没有预见到接下来会发生什么。

sents.en: A string of accidents, including the partial collapse of a cooling tower in 2007 and the discovery of an underground pipe system leakage, raised serious questions about both Vermont Yankee's safety and Entergy's management — especially after the company made misleading statements about the pipe.
sents.cn: 一连串事故——包括2007年一座冷却塔的部分坍塌，以及一处地下管道系统渗漏的发现——使人们对佛蒙特扬基核电站的安全性以及安特吉公司的管理水平都产生了严重的质疑，尤其是在该公司就管道问题作出误导性陈述之后。

sents.en: Enraged by Entergy's behavior, the Vermont Senate voted 26 to 4 last year against allowing an extension.
sents.cn: 佛蒙特州参议院对安特吉公司的行为深感愤怒，去年以26票对4票否决了延期运营的申请。

sents.en: Now the company is suddenly claiming that the 2002 agreement is invalid because of the 2006 legislation, and that only the federal government has regulatory power over nuclear issues.
sents.cn: 如今该公司却突然声称，鉴于2006年的立法，2002年的协议已不再有效，而且唯有联邦政府才对核问题拥有监管权力。

sents.en: The legal issues in the case are obscure: whereas the Supreme Court has ruled that states do have some regulatory authority over nuclear power, legal scholars say that Vermont case will offer a precedent-setting test of how far those powers extend.
sents.cn: 此案中的法律问题晦涩难明：尽管最高法院已裁定各州对核电确实拥有部分监管权，但法律学者指出，佛蒙特州一案将就这些权力的延伸范围究竟有多大，提供一次具有判例意义的检验。

sents.en: Certainly, there are valid concerns about the patchwork regulations that could result if every state sets its own rules.
sents.cn: 诚然，人们对于一旦每个州都各自制定规则而可能导致的法规杂乱无章，是怀有正当忧虑的。

sents.en: But had Entergy kept its word, that debate would be beside the point.
sents.cn: 但倘若安特吉公司当初信守了诺言，这一争论便无关紧要了。

sents.en: The company seems to have concluded that its reputation in Vermont is already so damaged that it has nothing left to lose by going to war with the state.
sents.cn: 这家公司似乎已经断定，它在佛蒙特州的声誉早已严重受损，以至于即便与该州开战，它也再无可失去的东西了。

sents.en: But there should be consequences.
sents.cn: 但理应承担相应的后果。

sents.en: Permission to run a nuclear plant is a public trust.
sents.cn: 运营一座核电站的许可是一种公众托付。

sents.en: Entergy runs 11 other reactors in the United States, including Pilgrim Nuclear station in Plymouth.
sents.cn: 安特吉公司在美国还运营着另外11座反应堆，其中包括位于普利茅斯的“朝圣者”核电站。

sents.en: Pledging to run Pilgrim safely, the company has applied for federal permission to keep it open for another 20 years.
sents.cn: 该公司承诺将安全运营“朝圣者”核电站，并已向联邦政府申请许可，以便使其再运营20年。

sents.en: But as the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) reviews the company's application, it should keep it mind what promises from Entergy are worth.
sents.cn: 但在核管理委员会（NRC）审查该公司的申请时，它应当牢记：安特吉公司的承诺究竟有几分分量。

sents.en: In the idealized version of how science is done, facts about the world are waiting to be observed and collected by objective researchers who use the scientific method to carry out their work.
sents.cn: 在科学研究方式的理想化版本中，关于世界的种种事实正等待着客观的研究者去观察和收集，这些研究者运用科学方法来开展自己的工作。

sents.en: But in the everyday practice of science, discovery frequently follows an ambiguous and complicated route.
sents.cn: 但在科学的日常实践中，发现往往遵循着一条含混而曲折的路径。

sents.en: We aim to be objective, but we cannot escape the context of our unique life experiences.
sents.cn: 我们力求客观，却无法摆脱自身独特生活经历所构成的背景。

sents.en: Prior knowledge and interests influence what we experience, what we think our experiences mean, and the subsequent actions we take.
sents.cn: 先前的知识和兴趣会影响我们体验到什么、我们认为这些体验意味着什么，以及我们随后采取的行动。

sents.en: Opportunities for misinterpretation, error, and self-deception abound.
sents.cn: 误读、谬误和自欺的可能性比比皆是。

sents.en: Consequently, discovery claims should be thought of as protoscience.
sents.cn: 因此，种种“发现”的主张应当被看作“原科学”。

sents.en: Similar to newly staked mining claims, they are full of potential.
sents.cn: 如同新近圈定的采矿权一样，它们充满了潜在的价值。

sents.en: But it takes collective scrutiny and acceptance to transform a discovery claim into a mature discovery.
sents.cn: 但要把一项“发现”的主张转化为一项成熟的发现，则需要集体的审视与认可。

sents.en: This is the credibility process, through which the individual researcher's me, here, now becomes the community's anyone, anywhere, anytime.
sents.cn: 这便是所谓的“可信化过程”，正是通过这一过程，研究者个人“此时此地的我”才变成了科学共同体“任何时间、任何地点的任何人”。

sents.en: Objective knowledge is the goal, not the starting point.
sents.cn: 客观知识是目标，而非起点。

sents.en: Once a discovery claim becomes public, the discoverer receives intellectual credit.
sents.cn: 一项“发现”的主张一旦公之于众，发现者便获得了知识上的荣誉。

sents.en: But, unlike with mining claims, the community takes control of what happens next.
sents.cn: 但与采矿权不同的是，此后事态的走向将由科学共同体来掌控。

sents.en: Within the complex social structure of the scientific community, researchers make discoveries; editors and reviewers act as gatekeepers by controlling the publication process; other scientists use the new finding to suit their own purposes; and finally, the public (including other scientists) receives the new discovery and possibly accompanying technology.
sents.cn: 在科学共同体复杂的社会结构之内，研究者作出发现；编辑和审稿人通过控制发表过程而充当“把关人”；其他科学家则将这一新发现用于各自的目的；最后，公众（包括其他科学家）接受这一新发现以及可能随之而来的技术。

sents.en: As a discovery claim works its way through the community, the interaction and confrontation between shared and competing beliefs about the science and the technology involved transforms an individual's discovery claim into the community's credible discovery.
sents.cn: 随着一项“发现”的主张在共同体中一路推进，围绕所涉科学与技术的共有信念和相互对立的信念之间发生的互动与交锋，便把个人的“发现”主张转化为共同体可信的发现。

sents.en: Two paradoxes exist throughout this credibility process.
sents.cn: 在这一可信化过程中，自始至终存在着两个悖论。

sents.en: First, scientific work tends to focus on some aspect of prevailing knowledge that is viewed as incomplete or incorrect.
sents.cn: 其一，科学工作往往着眼于现有知识中被视为不完整或不正确的某些方面。

sents.en: Little reward accompanies duplication and confirmation of what is already known and believed.
sents.cn: 对已知且已被认可之事进行重复和印证，几乎不会带来什么回报。

sents.en: The goal is new-search, not re-search.
sents.cn: 目标在于“新探”，而非“重研”。

sents.en: Not surprisingly, newly published discovery claims and credible discoveries that appear to be important and convincing will always be open to challenge and potential modification or refutation by future researchers.
sents.cn: 不足为奇的是，新发表的、看似重要而令人信服的“发现”主张和可信的发现，将永远面向未来研究者的质疑，以及可能的修正或反驳。

sents.en: Second, novelty itself frequently provokes disbelief.
sents.cn: 其二，新颖性本身往往会激起怀疑。

sents.en: Nobel Laureate and physiologist Albert Szent-Györgyi once described discovery as "seeing what everybody has seen and thinking what nobody has thought."
sents.cn: 诺贝尔奖得主、生理学家阿尔伯特·圣捷尔吉曾把发现描述为“看见人人都看见过的东西，却想到无人想到过的东西”。

sents.en: But thinking what nobody else has thought and telling others what they have missed may not change their views.
sents.cn: 但是，想到他人未曾想到的东西，并告诉别人他们忽略了什么，未必就能改变他们的看法。

sents.en: Sometimes years are required for truly novel discovery claims to be accepted and appreciated.
sents.cn: 有时，真正新颖的“发现”主张需要经过数年才被接受和赏识。

sents.en: In the end, credibility "happens" to a discovery claim — a process that corresponds to what philosopher Annette Baier has described as the commons of the mind.
sents.cn: 归根结底，可信性是“降临”到一项“发现”主张之上的——这一过程恰与哲学家安妮特·拜尔所描述的“心智的公共领域”相吻合。

sents.en: "We reason together, challenge, revise, and complete each other's reasoning and each other's conceptions of reason."
sents.cn: “我们一同推理，相互质疑、修正和完善彼此的推理，以及彼此对理性的理解。”

sents.en: If the trade unionist Jimmy Hoffa were alive today, he would probably represent civil servants.
sents.cn: 如果工会活动家吉米·霍法今天还在世，他很可能会代表公务员群体。

sents.en: When Hoffa's Teamsters were in their prime in 1960, only one in ten American government workers belonged to a union; now 36% do.
sents.cn: 1960年霍法领导的卡车司机工会鼎盛之时，美国政府雇员中只有十分之一加入了工会；而如今这一比例已达36%。

sents.en: In 2009 the number of unionists in America's public sector passed that of their fellow members in the private sector.
sents.cn: 2009年，美国公共部门的工会会员人数超过了私营部门的同行。

sents.en: In Britain, more than half of public-sector workers but only about 15% of private-sector ones are unionized.
sents.cn: 在英国，半数以上的公共部门员工加入了工会，而私营部门的这一比例却仅有约15%。

sents.en: There are three reasons for the public-sector unions' thriving.
sents.cn: 公共部门工会之所以兴盛，有三个原因。

sents.en: First, they can shut things down without suffering much in the way of consequences.
sents.cn: 第一，他们可以让各项事务停摆，自己却不会因此承受太大的后果。

sents.en: Second, they are mostly bright and well-educated.
sents.cn: 第二，他们大多聪明且受过良好教育。

sents.en: A quarter of America's public-sector workers have a university degree.
sents.cn: 美国公共部门员工中有四分之一拥有大学学位。

sents.en: Third, they now dominate left-of-centre politics.
sents.cn: 第三，他们如今主导着中左翼政治。

sents.en: Some of their ties go back a long way.
sents.cn: 他们的某些渊源由来已久。

sents.en: Britain's Labor Party, as its name implies, has long been associated with trade unionism.
sents.cn: 英国工党，正如其名称所暗示的那样，长期以来一直与工会运动密切相关。

sents.en: Its current leader, Ed Miliband, owes his position to votes from public-sector unions.
sents.cn: 其现任领袖埃德·米利班德的领导地位正是得益于公共部门工会的选票。

sents.en: At the state level their influence can be even more fearsome.
sents.cn: 在州一级，他们的影响力甚至可能更加可畏。

sents.en: Mark Baldassare of the Public Policy Institute of California points out that much of the state's budget is patrolled by unions.
sents.cn: 加利福尼亚州公共政策研究所的马克·巴尔达萨雷指出，该州预算的很大一部分都受到工会的掌控。

sents.en: The teachers' unions keep an eye on schools, the CCPOA on prisons and a variety of labor groups on health care.
sents.cn: 教师工会盯着学校，加州惩教与教养人员协会（CCPOA）盯着监狱，形形色色的劳工团体则盯着医疗保健领域。

sents.en: In many rich countries average wages in the state sector are higher than in the private one.
sents.cn: 在许多富裕国家，公共部门的平均工资都高于私营部门。

sents.en: But the real gains come in benefits and work practices.
sents.cn: 但真正的实惠却体现在福利待遇和工作惯例上。

sents.en: Politicians have repeatedly "backloaded" public-sector pay deals, keeping the pay increases modest but adding to holidays and especially pensions that are already generous.
sents.cn: 政界人士一再对公共部门的薪酬协议采取“后置”做法，把工资涨幅压得不大，却增加假期，尤其是增加本已十分优厚的养老金。

sents.en: Reform has been vigorously opposed, perhaps most egregiously in education, where charter schools, academies and merit pay all faced drawn-out battles.
sents.cn: 改革一直遭到激烈反对，其中或许以教育领域最为离谱——在这一领域，特许学校、自治学校和绩效工资都经历了旷日持久的较量。

sents.en: Even though there is plenty of evidence that the quality of the teachers is the most important variable, teachers' unions have fought against getting rid of bad ones and promoting good ones.
sents.cn: 尽管有大量证据表明教师的素质是最重要的变量，教师工会却一直反对辞退不称职的教师、提拔优秀的教师。

sents.en: As the cost to everyone else has become clearer, politicians have begun to clamp down.
sents.cn: 随着其他所有人为此付出的代价日益清晰，政界人士已开始加以遏制。

sents.en: In Wisconsin the unions have rallied thousands of supporters against Scott Walker, the hardline Republican governor.
sents.cn: 在威斯康星州，工会已动员数千名支持者，反对立场强硬的共和党州长斯科特·沃克。

sents.en: But many within the public sector suffer under the current system, too.
sents.cn: 但公共部门内部也有许多人在现行体制下深受其苦。

sents.en: John Donahue at Harvard's Kennedy School points out that the norms of culture in Western civil services suit those who want to stay put but is bad for high achievers.
sents.cn: 哈佛大学肯尼迪学院的约翰·多纳休指出，西方公务员体系中的文化规范，适合那些想要安于现状的人，对成绩突出者却很不利。

sents.en: The only American public-sector workers who earn well above $250,000 a year are university sports coaches and the president of the United States.
sents.cn: 美国公共部门员工中，年薪远超25万美元的，唯有大学体育教练和美国总统。

sents.en: Bankers' fat pay packets have attracted much criticism, but a public-sector system that does not reward high achievers may be a much bigger problem for America.
sents.cn: 银行家们丰厚的薪酬已招致了诸多批评，但一个不奖励成绩突出者的公共部门体制，对美国而言或许是一个大得多的问题。

sents.en: In the 2006 film version of The Devil Wears Prada, Miranda Priestly, played by Meryl Streep, scolds her unattractive assistant for imagining that high fashion doesn't affect her.
sents.cn: 在2006年的电影版《穿普拉达的女魔头》中，梅丽尔·斯特里普饰演的米兰达·普里斯特利训斥她那位其貌不扬的助理，因为后者竟以为高级时装与自己毫不相干。

sents.en: Priestly explains how the deep blue color of the assistant's sweater descended over the years from fashion shows to department stores and to the bargain bin in which the poor girl doubtless found her garment.
sents.cn: 普里斯特利向她解释，这位助理身上那件深蓝色毛衣的颜色，是如何在数年间从时装秀逐级流传到百货商场，最终流入折扣货筐——而这位可怜的姑娘无疑正是在那里淘到了这件衣服。

sents.en: This top-down conception of the fashion business couldn't be more out of date or at odds with the feverish world described in Overdressed, Elizabeth Cline's three-year indictment of "fast fashion".
sents.cn: 这种自上而下看待时装业的观念，已经过时得不能再过时，也与伊丽莎白·克莱恩在《过度着装》一书中所描绘的那个狂热世界格格不入；这本书是她历时三年对“快时尚”的声讨。

sents.en: In the last decade or so, advances in technology have allowed mass-market labels such as Zara, H&M, and Uniqlo to react to trends more quickly and anticipate demand more precisely.
sents.cn: 在过去十年左右的时间里，技术的进步使Zara、H&M和优衣库等大众品牌能够更快速地对潮流作出反应，并更精准地预判需求。

sents.en: Quicker turnarounds mean less wasted inventory, more frequent releases, and more profit.
sents.cn: 更快的周转意味着库存浪费更少、新品上市更频繁、利润更丰厚。

sents.en: These labels encourage style-conscious consumers to see clothes as disposable—meant to last only a wash or two, although they don't advertise that—and to renew their wardrobe every few weeks.
sents.cn: 这些品牌怂恿讲究穿着的消费者把衣服视为一次性用品——尽管它们不会公开宣传，这些衣服实际上只经得起洗一两次——并且每隔几周就更新一次衣橱。

sents.en: By offering on-trend items at dirt-cheap prices, Cline argues, these brands have hijacked fashion cycles, shaking an industry long accustomed to a seasonal pace.
sents.cn: 克莱恩认为，这些品牌以低得离谱的价格提供紧跟潮流的商品，从而劫持了时尚的循环周期，撼动了一个长期习惯于按季节节奏运作的行业。

sents.en: The victims of this revolution, of course, are not limited to designers.
sents.cn: 当然，这场变革的受害者并不仅限于设计师。

sents.en: For H&M to offer a $5.95 knit miniskirt in all its 2,300-plus stores around the world, it must rely on low-wage overseas labor, order in volumes that strain natural resources, and use massive amounts of harmful chemicals.
sents.cn: H&M要想在其遍布全球的两千三百多家门店里出售一条售价5.95美元的针织迷你裙，就必须依赖海外的低薪劳动力，下单量之大使自然资源不堪重负，还要使用大量有害化学物质。

sents.en: Overdressed is the fashion world's answer to consumer-activist bestsellers like Michael Pollan's The Omnivore's Dilemma.
sents.cn: 《过度着装》是时尚界对消费者维权类畅销书的回应，可与迈克尔·波伦的《杂食者的两难》相提并论。

sents.en: "Mass-produced clothing, like fast food, fills a hunger and need, yet is non-durable and wasteful," Cline argues.
sents.cn: 克莱恩指出：“批量生产的服装就像快餐一样，满足了人们的饥渴与需求，却既不耐用又造成浪费。”

sents.en: Americans, she finds, buy roughly 20 billion garments a year—about 64 items per person—and no matter how much they give away, this excess leads to waste.
sents.cn: 她发现，美国人每年大约购买二百亿件衣物——人均约六十四件——而无论他们捐出多少，这种过剩都会导致浪费。

sents.en: Towards the end of Overdressed, Cline introduced her ideal, a Brooklyn woman named Sarah Kate Beaumont, who since 2008 has made all of her own clothes—and beautifully.
sents.cn: 在《过度着装》一书的结尾，克莱恩介绍了她心目中的理想人物：布鲁克林一位名叫莎拉·凯特·博蒙特的女士，她自2008年起便亲手缝制自己所有的衣服——而且做得相当漂亮。

sents.en: But as Cline is the first to note, it took Beaumont decades to perfect her craft; her example can't be knocked off.
sents.cn: 但正如克莱恩第一个指出的那样，博蒙特花了数十年才把这门手艺练得炉火纯青；她的榜样无法被照搬复制。

sents.en: Though several fast-fashion companies have made efforts to curb their impact on labor and the environment—including H&M, with its green Conscious Collection line—Cline believes lasting change can only be effected by the customer.
sents.cn: 尽管包括H&M在内的几家快时尚公司已为减少其对劳工和环境的影响做出了努力——比如H&M推出了环保的“良心系列”——但克莱恩相信，持久的改变只能由消费者来促成。

sents.en: She exhibits the idealism common to many advocates of sustainability, be it in food or in energy.
sents.cn: 她表现出许多可持续发展倡导者所共有的理想主义，无论是在食品还是能源领域。

sents.en: Vanity is a constant; people will only start shopping more sustainably when they can't afford not to.
sents.cn: 虚荣是一种不变的人性；只有当人们再也无力承担挥霍的代价时，才会开始更可持续地购物。

sents.en: An old saying has it that half of all advertising budgets are wasted—the trouble is, no one knows which half.
sents.cn: 有一句老话说，所有广告预算中有一半都被浪费了——麻烦在于，没人知道是哪一半。

sents.en: In the internet age, at least in theory, this fraction can be much reduced.
sents.cn: 在互联网时代，至少在理论上，这一比例可以大大降低。

sents.en: By watching what people search for, click on and say online, companies can aim "behavioral" ads at those most likely to buy.
sents.cn: 通过观察人们在网上搜索什么、点击什么、谈论什么，企业可以把“行为定向”广告投放给那些最有可能购买的人。

sents.en: In the past couple of weeks a quarrel has illustrated the value to advertisers of such fine-grained information: Should advertisers assume that people are happy to be tracked and sent behavioral ads?
sents.cn: 在过去几周里，一场争论恰好说明了此类精细信息对广告商的价值：广告商是否应当假定人们乐于被追踪并接收行为定向广告？

sents.en: Or should they have explicit permission?
sents.cn: 还是说，他们应当事先取得明确的许可？

sents.en: In December 2010 America's Federal Trade Commission (FTC) proposed adding a "do not track" (DNT) option to internet browsers, so that users could tell advertisers that they did not want to be followed.
sents.cn: 2010年12月，美国联邦贸易委员会（FTC）提议在互联网浏览器中加入一个“禁止追踪”（DNT）选项，以便用户可以告知广告商自己不愿被追踪。

sents.en: Microsoft's Internet Explorer and Apple's Safari both offer DNT; Google's Chrome is due to do so this year.
sents.cn: 微软的Internet Explorer和苹果的Safari都已提供DNT功能；谷歌的Chrome也将在今年提供。

sents.en: In February the FTC and Digital Advertising Alliance (DAA) agreed that the industry would get cracking on responding to DNT requests.
sents.cn: 今年2月，联邦贸易委员会与数字广告联盟（DAA）达成一致，业界将着手响应DNT请求。

sents.en: On May 31st Microsoft set off the row: It said that InternetExplorer 10, the version due to appear Windows 8, would have DNT as a default.
sents.cn: 5月31日，微软引发了这场争论：它表示，随Windows 8一同发布的Internet Explorer 10将默认开启DNT。

sents.en: Advertisers are horrified.
sents.cn: 广告商们惊恐万分。

sents.en: Human nature being what it is, most people stick with default settings.
sents.cn: 鉴于人性使然，大多数人都会沿用默认设置。

sents.en: Few switch DNT on now, but if tracking is off it will stay off.
sents.cn: 如今很少有人主动开启DNT，但如果追踪功能默认关闭，它就会一直保持关闭。

sents.en: Bob Liodice, the chief executive of the Association of National Advertisers, says consumers will be worse off if the industry cannot collect information about their preferences.
sents.cn: 美国全国广告商协会的首席执行官鲍勃·利奥迪斯表示，如果业界无法收集消费者的偏好信息，受损的将是消费者。

sents.en: People will not get fewer ads, he says, "they'll get less meaningful, less targeted ads."
sents.cn: 他说，人们看到的广告不会减少，“而是会收到意义更小、针对性更弱的广告”。

sents.en: It is not yet clear how advertisers will respond.
sents.cn: 广告商将如何应对，目前尚不明朗。

sents.en: Getting a DNT signal does not oblige anyone to stop tracking, although some companies have promised to do so.
sents.cn: 收到DNT信号并不强制任何人停止追踪，尽管有些公司已承诺会这样做。

sents.en: Unable to tell whether someone really objects to behavioral ads or whether they are sticking with Microsoft's default, some may ignore a DNT signal and press on anyway.
sents.cn: 由于无法判断某人究竟是真的反对行为定向广告，还是仅仅沿用了微软的默认设置，有些公司可能会无视DNT信号，照旧追踪下去。

sents.en: Also unclear is why Microsoft has gone it alone.
sents.cn: 同样令人不解的是，微软为何要单独行动。

sents.en: After all, it has an ad business too, which it says will comply with DNT requests, though it is still working out how.
sents.cn: 毕竟，它自己也有广告业务，它表示该业务将遵从DNT请求，只是仍在研究具体如何操作。

sents.en: If it is trying to upset Google, which relies almost wholly on advertising, it has chosen an indirect method: There is no guarantee that DNT by default will become the norm.DNT does not seem an obviously huge selling point for Windows 8—though the firm has compared some of its other products favorably with Google's on that count before.
sents.cn: 如果它意在打击几乎完全依赖广告的谷歌，那么它选择的是一种迂回的方式：无法保证默认开启DNT会成为常态；对Windows 8而言，DNT似乎也算不上一个显而易见的巨大卖点——尽管该公司此前曾在这一点上拿自家其他产品与谷歌相比、并自夸更胜一筹。

sents.en: Brendon Lynch, Microsoft's chief privacy officer, blogged: "we believe consumers should have more control."
sents.cn: 微软首席隐私官布伦顿·林奇在博客中写道：“我们认为消费者应当拥有更多的控制权。”

sents.en: Could it really be that simple?
sents.cn: 事情真会这么简单吗？

sents.en: Up until a few decades ago, our visions of the future were largely—though by no means uniformly—glowingly positive.
sents.cn: 直到几十年前，我们对未来的设想在很大程度上还是——尽管绝非千篇一律——充满乐观、一片光明的。

sents.en: Science and technology would cure all the ills of humanity, leading to lives of fulfillment and opportunity for all.
sents.cn: 科学与技术将治愈人类的一切弊病，让所有人都能过上充实而充满机遇的生活。

sents.en: Now utopia has grown unfashionable, as we have gained a deeper appreciation of the range of threats facing us, from asteroid strike to epidemic flu to climate change.
sents.cn: 如今，乌托邦已不再时兴，因为我们对自身所面临的种种威胁有了更深刻的认识，从小行星撞击到流感肆虐，再到气候变化。

sents.en: You might even be tempted to assume that humanity has little future to look forward to.
sents.cn: 你甚至可能不禁会以为，人类已没有多少未来可以期盼了。

sents.en: But such gloominess is misplaced.
sents.cn: 但这种悲观情绪是没有道理的。

sents.en: The fossil record shows that many species have endured for millions of years—so why shouldn't we?
sents.cn: 化石记录表明，许多物种已经延续了数百万年——那么我们为什么不能呢？

sents.en: Take a broader look at our species' place in the universe, and it becomes clear that we have an excellent chance of surviving for tens, if not hundreds, of thousands of years.
sents.cn: 如果把眼光放宽一些，去审视我们这一物种在宇宙中所处的位置，就会清楚地看到，我们极有可能再存续数万年，乃至数十万年。

sents.en: Look up Homo sapiens in the "Red List" of threatened species of the international Union for the Concentration of Nature (IUCN), and you will read: "Listed as Least Concern as the species is very widely distributed, adaptable, currently increasing, and there are no major threats resulting in an overall population decline."
sents.cn: 在世界自然保护联盟（IUCN）的濒危物种“红色名录”中查一查智人，你会读到这样的话：“被列为‘无危’，因为该物种分布极广、适应力强、目前数量正在增长，且不存在导致其总体数量下降的重大威胁。”

sents.en: So what does our deep future hold?
sents.cn: 那么，我们遥远的未来将会如何呢？

sents.en: A growing number of researchers and organizations are now thinking seriously about that question.
sents.cn: 如今，越来越多的研究者和机构开始认真思考这个问题。

sents.en: For example, the Long Now Foundation has as its flagship project a mechanical clock that is designed to still be marking time thousands of years hence.
sents.cn: 例如，“今日永存基金会”的旗舰项目便是一座机械时钟，其设计目标是在数千年之后仍能继续走时计时。

sents.en: Perhaps willfully, it may be easier to think about such lengthy timescales than about the more immediate future.
sents.cn: 或许是出于刻意，思考这样漫长的时间尺度，反倒可能比思考更切近的未来更容易。

sents.en: The potential evolution of today's technology, and its social consequences, is dazzlingly complicated, and it's perhaps best left to science-fiction writers and futurologists to explore the many possibilities we can envisage.
sents.cn: 当今技术的潜在演变及其社会后果错综复杂、令人眼花缭乱，要去探索我们所能设想的种种可能性，这件事或许最好还是交给科幻作家和未来学家去做。

sents.en: That's one reason why we have launched Arc, a new publication dedicated to the near future.
sents.cn: 这正是我们创办《弧》的原因之一，这是一份专注于近未来的全新刊物。

sents.en: But take a longer view and there is a surprising amount that we can say with considerable assurance.
sents.cn: 但只要把眼光放长远一些，就会发现有许多事情是我们能够相当有把握地断言的。

sents.en: As so often, the past holds the key to the future: we have now identified enough of the long-term patterns shaping the history of the planet, and our species, to make evidence-based forecasts about the situations in which our descendants will find themselves.
sents.cn: 正如常常出现的情形，过去掌握着通向未来的钥匙：我们如今已经辨识出足够多塑造这颗星球及我们这一物种历史的长期规律，从而能够对我们的后代将身处何种境况作出有据可依的预测。

sents.en: This long perspective makes the pessimistic view of our prospects seem more likely to be a passing fad.
sents.cn: 这种长远的眼光使得对我们前景的悲观看法更像是一种转瞬即逝的流行风潮。

sents.en: To be sure, the future is not all rosy.
sents.cn: 诚然，未来并非一片美好。

sents.en: But we are now knowledgeable enough to reduce many of the risks that threatened the existence of earlier humans, and to improve the lot of those to come.
sents.cn: 但我们如今已掌握了足够的知识，足以降低许多曾威胁早期人类生存的风险，并改善后代的境遇。

sents.en: On a five to three vote, the Supreme Court knocked out much of Arizona's immigration law Monday—a modest policy victory for the Obama Administration.
sents.cn: 周一，最高法院以五比三的投票结果推翻了亚利桑那州移民法的大部分内容——这对奥巴马政府而言是一场不大不小的政策胜利。

sents.en: But on the more important matter of the Constitution, the decision was an 8-0 defeat for the Administration's effort to upset the balance of power between the federal government and the states.
sents.cn: 但在更为重要的宪法问题上，这一裁决却是政府的一次八比零惨败，使其打破联邦政府与各州之间权力平衡的图谋落空。

sents.en: In Arizona v. United States, the majority overturned three of the four contested provisions of Arizona's controversial plan to have state and local police enforce federal immigration law.
sents.cn: 在“亚利桑那州诉合众国”一案中，多数派法官推翻了亚利桑那州那项有争议方案的四条受质疑条款中的三条；该方案旨在让州和地方警察执行联邦移民法。

sents.en: The Constitutional principles that Washington alone has the power to "establish a uniform Rule of Naturalization" and that federal laws precede state laws are noncontroversial.
sents.cn: 唯有联邦政府才有权“确立统一的归化规则”，以及联邦法律高于州法律，这两条宪法原则是没有争议的。

sents.en: Arizona had attempted to fashion state policies that ran parallel to the existing federal ones.
sents.cn: 亚利桑那州此前试图制定一些与现行联邦政策并行的州政策。

sents.en: Justice Anthony Kennedy, joined by Chief Justice John Roberts and the Court's liberals, ruled that the state flew too close to the federal sun.
sents.cn: 大法官安东尼·肯尼迪，连同首席大法官约翰·罗伯茨以及最高法院的自由派法官一道裁定，该州过于靠近联邦这个“太阳”了。

sents.en: On the overturned provisions the majority held that Congress had deliberately "occupied the field"and Arizona has thus intruded on the federal's privileged powers.
sents.cn: 对于那些被推翻的条款，多数派法官认为，国会已蓄意“占据了这一领域”，因而亚利桑那州侵犯了联邦政府的专属权力。

sents.en: However, the Justices said that Arizona police would be allowed to verify the legal status of people who come in contact with law enforcement.
sents.cn: 不过，大法官们表示，亚利桑那州警察将被允许核查那些与执法部门发生接触者的合法身份。

sents.en: That's because Congress has always envisioned joint federal-state immigration enforcement and explicitly encourages state officers to share information and cooperate with federal colleagues.
sents.cn: 这是因为国会一直设想由联邦与各州共同执行移民法，并明确鼓励州级官员与联邦同行分享信息、相互协作。

sents.en: Two of the three objecting Justices—Samuel Alito and Clarence Thomas—agreed with this Constitutional logic but disagreed about which Arizona rules conflicted with the federal statute.
sents.cn: 三位持异议的大法官中有两位——塞缪尔·阿利托和克拉伦斯·托马斯——认同上述宪法逻辑，但在亚利桑那州的哪些规定与联邦法规相冲突这一点上意见相左。

sents.en: The only major objection came from Justice Antonin Scalia, who offered an even more robust defense of state privileges going back to the Alien and Sedition Acts.
sents.cn: 唯一重大的异议来自大法官安东宁·斯卡利亚，他对州权提出了更为有力的辩护，其依据甚至可以追溯到《客籍法和惩治煽动叛乱法》。

sents.en: The 8-0 objection to President Obama turns on what Justice Samuel Alito describes in his objection as "a shocking assertion of federal executive power".
sents.cn: 对奥巴马总统这八比零的反对意见，焦点在于大法官塞缪尔·阿利托在其异议中所称的“一种骇人听闻地宣称联邦行政权力的主张”。

sents.en: The White House argued that Arizona's laws conflicted with its enforcement priorities, even if state laws complied with federal statutes to the letter.
sents.cn: 白宫辩称，即便各州法律一字不差地符合联邦法规，亚利桑那州的法律仍与联邦的执法优先事项相冲突。

sents.en: In effect, the White House claimed that it could invalidate any otherwise legitimate state law that it disagrees with.
sents.cn: 实际上，白宫声称它可以宣布任何一项本属合法、但它不赞同的州法律无效。

sents.en: Some powers do belong exclusively to the federal government, and control of citizenship and the borders is among them.
sents.cn: 诚然，有些权力确实专属于联邦政府，对公民身份和边境的管控便在其列。

sents.en: But if Congress wanted to prevent states from using their own resources to check immigration status, it could.
sents.cn: 但是，如果国会想要阻止各州动用自身资源去核查移民身份，它本可以这么做。

sents.en: It never did so.
sents.cn: 可它从未这样做过。

sents.en: The Administration was in essence asserting that because it didn't want to carry out Congress's immigration wishes, no state should be allowed to do so either.
sents.cn: 说到底，政府的主张无非是：因为它自己不愿执行国会在移民问题上的意愿，所以也不应允许任何一个州去执行。

sents.en: Every Justice rightly rejected this remarkable claim.
sents.cn: 每一位大法官都理所应当地驳回了这一惊人的主张。

sents.en: In order to "change lives for the better" and reduce "dependency," George Osborne, Chancellor of the Exchequer, introduced the "upfront work search" scheme.
sents.cn: 为了“让生活变得更美好”并减少“依赖”，英国财政大臣乔治·奥斯本推出了“预先求职”计划。

sents.en: Only if the jobless arrive at the job centre with a CV register for online job search, and start looking for work will they be eligible for benefit-and then they should report weekly rather than fortnightly.
sents.cn: 失业者只有带着简历来到就业中心、注册在线求职并开始找工作，才有资格领取救济金——而且此后他们必须每周而非每两周前来报到一次。

sents.en: What could be more reasonable?
sents.cn: 还有什么比这更合情合理的呢？

sents.en: More apparent reasonableness followed.
sents.cn: 随后又出现了更多看似合理的举措。

sents.en: There will now be a seven-day wait for the jobseeker's allowance.
sents.cn: 如今申领求职者津贴要先等待七天。

sents.en: "Those first few days should be spent looking for work, not looking to sign on." he claimed.
sents.cn: 他声称：“最初的那几天应当用来找工作，而不是用来登记领取救济。”

sents.en: "We're doing these things because we know they help people say off benefits and help those on benefits get into work faster"
sents.cn: “我们之所以推行这些举措，是因为我们知道它们能帮助人们摆脱对救济金的依赖，并帮助那些领取救济金的人更快地找到工作。”

sents.en: Help?
sents.cn: 帮助？

sents.en: Really?
sents.cn: 真的吗？

sents.en: On first hearing, this was the socially concerned chancellor, trying to change lives for the better, complete with "reforms" to an obviously indulgent system that demands too little effort from the newly unemployed to find work, and subsides laziness.
sents.cn: 乍一听，这位财政大臣关心社会民生，努力让人们的生活变得更美好，还对一套显然过于纵容的制度进行了“改革”——这套制度对新近失业者的求职努力要求太低，等于在补贴懒惰。

sents.en: What motivated him, we were to understand, was his zeal for "fundamental fairness"-protecting the taxpayer, controlling spending and ensuring that only the most deserving claimants received their benefits.
sents.cn: 我们应当明白，驱使他这样做的，是他对“根本公平”的热忱——保护纳税人、控制开支，并确保只有最应当获得救济的申领者才能领到救济金。

sents.en: Losing a job is hurting: you don't skip down to the jobcentre with a song in your heart, delighted at the prospect of doubling your income from the generous state.
sents.cn: 失业是痛苦的：你不会心怀喜悦、哼着小曲蹦跳着走向就业中心，为有望从慷慨的国家那里把收入翻一番而欣喜。

sents.en: It is financially terrifying, psychologically embarrassing and you know that support is minimal and extraordinarily hard to get.
sents.cn: 失业在经济上令人惶恐，在心理上令人难堪，而且你知道能得到的支持微乎其微、极难争取。

sents.en: You are now not wanted; you are now excluded from the work environment that offers purpose and structure in your life.
sents.cn: 如今你不再被需要；你被排斥在那个为你的人生提供目标和秩序的工作环境之外。

sents.en: Worse, the crucial income to feed yourself and your family and pay the bills has disappeared.
sents.cn: 更糟的是，那份用来养活自己和家人、支付各种账单的至关重要的收入也没有了。

sents.en: Ask anyone newly unemployed what they want and the answer is always: a job.
sents.cn: 随便问一个新近失业的人想要什么，答案永远是：一份工作。

sents.en: But in Osborneland, your first instinct is to fall into dependency- permanent dependency if you can get it-supported by a state only too ready to indulge your falsehood.
sents.cn: 但在奥斯本的世界里，你的第一反应却是陷入依赖——如果能办到，最好是永久的依赖——靠一个一味乐于纵容你弄虚作假的国家来供养。

sents.en: It is as though 20 years of ever- tougher reforms of the job search and benefit administration system never happened.
sents.cn: 这仿佛是二十年来对求职和救济金管理制度日益严苛的改革从未发生过一样。

sents.en: The principle of British welfare is no longer that you can insure yourself against the risk of unemployment and receive unconditional payments if the disaster happens.
sents.cn: 英国福利的原则不再是：你可以为失业风险投保，一旦不幸降临便能无条件地领取救济金。

sents.en: Even the very phrase 'jobseeker's allowance'-invented in 1996- is about redefining the unemployed as a "jobseeker" who had no mandatory right to a benefit he or she has earned through making national insurance contributions.
sents.cn: 甚至连“求职者津贴”这个1996年发明出来的说法本身，也是在把失业者重新界定为“求职者”——此人对自己通过缴纳国民保险费而挣得的救济金并不享有强制性的领取权。

sents.en: Instead, the claimant receives a time-limited "allowance," conditional on actively seeking a job; no entitlement and no insurance, at £71.70 a week, one of the least generous in the EU.
sents.cn: 相反，申领者领到的是一笔有时限的“津贴”，前提是积极求职；既无领取资格，也无保险性质，每周71.70英镑，是欧盟中最不慷慨的津贴之一。

sents.en: All around the world, lawyers generate more hostility than the members of any other profession---with the possible exception of journalism.
sents.cn: 在世界各地，律师招致的敌意比任何其他职业的从业者都要多——也许只有新闻业是个例外。

sents.en: But there are few places where clients have more grounds for complaint than America.
sents.cn: 但很少有哪个地方像美国这样，让客户有如此多的理由抱怨。

sents.en: During the decade before the economic crisis, spending on legal services in America grew twice as fast as inflation.
sents.cn: 在经济危机爆发前的十年里，美国法律服务支出的增长速度是通货膨胀的两倍。

sents.en: The best lawyers made skyscrapers-full of money, tempting ever more students to pile into law schools.
sents.cn: 最顶尖的律师赚得盆满钵满，赚的钱足以堆满摩天大楼，吸引越来越多的学生涌入法学院。

sents.en: But most law graduates never get a big-firm job.
sents.cn: 但大多数法学院毕业生从未能进入大型律所工作。

sents.en: Many of them instead become the kind of nuisance-lawsuit filer that makes the tort system a costly nightmare.
sents.cn: 相反，他们中的许多人成了那种专门提起无谓诉讼的人，使侵权诉讼制度沦为一场代价高昂的噩梦。

sents.en: There are many reasons for this.
sents.cn: 造成这种状况的原因有很多。

sents.en: One is the excessive costs of a legal education.
sents.cn: 其一是法学教育费用过高。

sents.en: There is just one path for a lawyer in most American states: a four-year undergraduate degree in some unrelated subject,then a three-year law degree at one of 200 law schools authorized by the American Bar Association and an expensive preparation for the bar exam.
sents.cn: 在美国大多数州，要成为律师只有一条路：先在某个不相关的专业读完四年本科，然后到美国律师协会认可的200所法学院之一攻读三年法学学位，再为律师资格考试做一番花费不菲的备考。

sents.en: This leaves today's average law-school graduate with $100,000 of debt on top of undergraduate debts.
sents.cn: 这使得如今普通的法学院毕业生在本科阶段的债务之外，又背上了10万美元的债务。

sents.en: Law-school debt means that they have to work fearsomely hard.
sents.cn: 法学院的债务意味着他们不得不拼命苦干。

sents.en: Reforming the system would help both lawyers and their customers.
sents.cn: 改革这一制度对律师和他们的客户都有好处。

sents.en: Sensible ideas have been around for a long time, but the state-level bodies that govern the profession have been too conservative to implement them.
sents.cn: 明智的改革设想早已存在，但管理这一行业的各州级机构过于保守，迟迟不肯将其付诸实施。

sents.en: One idea is to allow people to study law as an undergraduate degree.
sents.cn: 一种设想是允许人们把法律作为本科专业来学习。

sents.en: Another is to let students sit for the bar after only two years of law school.
sents.cn: 另一种设想是让学生只读两年法学院就可以参加律师资格考试。

sents.en: If the bar exam is truly a stern enough test for a would-be lawyer, those who can sit it earlier should be allowed to do so.
sents.cn: 如果说律师资格考试对一名准律师而言确实是一项足够严格的检验，那么能够更早参加考试的人就应当被允许这样做。

sents.en: Students who do not need the extra training could cut their debt mountain by a third.
sents.cn: 那些并不需要额外培训的学生，可以借此把堆积如山的债务削减三分之一。

sents.en: The other reason why costs are so high is the restrictive guild-like ownership structure of the business.
sents.cn: 费用如此之高的另一个原因，是这一行业类似行会的、带有限制性的所有权结构。

sents.en: Except in the District of Columbia, non-lawyers may not own any share of a law firm.
sents.cn: 除哥伦比亚特区外，非律师不得持有律所的任何股份。

sents.en: This keeps fees high and innovation slow.
sents.cn: 这使得收费居高不下、创新步履缓慢。

sents.en: There is pressure for change from within the profession, but opponents of change among the regulators insist that keeping outsiders out of a law firm isolates lawyers from the pressure to make money rather than serve clients ethically.
sents.cn: 行业内部要求变革的呼声不断，但监管者中反对变革的人坚持认为，把外人挡在律所之外能使律师免受赚钱压力的影响，从而恪守职业道德为客户服务，而非一味逐利。

sents.en: In fact, allowing non-lawyers to own shares in law firms would reduce costs and improve services to customers, by encouraging law firms to use technology and to employ professional managers to focus on improving firms' efficiency.
sents.cn: 事实上，允许非律师持有律所股份将会降低成本、改善对客户的服务，因为这能促使律所运用技术，并聘请专业管理人员专注于提升律所的运营效率。

sents.en: After all, other countries, such as Australia and Britain, have started liberalizing their legal professions.
sents.cn: 毕竟，澳大利亚和英国等其他国家已经开始放开对法律行业的管制。

sents.en: America should follow.
sents.cn: 美国理应效仿。

sents.en: The US$3-million Fundamental physics prize is indeed an interesting experiment, as Alexander Polyakov said when he accepted this year's award in March.
sents.cn: 正如亚历山大·波利亚科夫今年三月领取该奖项时所说，奖金高达300万美元的“基础物理学奖”的确是一项有趣的尝试。

sents.en: And it is far from the only one of its type.
sents.cn: 而且它远非这类奖项中唯一的一个。

sents.en: As a News Feature article in Nature discusses, a string of lucrative awards for researchers have joined the Nobel Prizes in recent years.
sents.cn: 正如《自然》杂志一篇新闻特写所讨论的那样，近年来一系列奖金丰厚的研究奖项与诺贝尔奖并列于世。

sents.en: Many, like the Fundamental Physics Prize, are funded from the telephone-number-sized bank accounts of Internet entrepreneurs.
sents.cn: 其中许多奖项，如基础物理学奖，资金来自互联网企业家那些数字长得像电话号码一样的银行账户。

sents.en: These benefactors have succeeded in their chosen fields, they say, and they want to use their wealth to draw attention to those who have succeeded in science.
sents.cn: 这些捐助者说，他们已在各自选定的领域取得成功，如今想用自己的财富来引起人们对那些在科学上取得成功者的关注。

sents.en: What's not to like?
sents.cn: 这有什么不好呢？

sents.en: Quite a lot, according to a handful of scientists quoted in the News Feature.
sents.cn: 据新闻特写中援引的少数几位科学家所言，不好之处还真不少。

sents.en: You cannot buy class, as the old saying goes, and these upstart entrepreneurs cannot buy their prizes the prestige of the Nobels.
sents.cn: 正如那句老话所说，格调是买不来的，这些暴发户般的企业家也无法为自己设立的奖项买来诺贝尔奖那样的声望。

sents.en: The new awards are an exercise in self-promotion for those behind them, say scientists.
sents.cn: 科学家们说，这些新设的奖项是其幕后出资者自我宣传的手段。

sents.en: They could distort the achievement-based system of peer-review-led research.
sents.cn: 它们可能扭曲那套以成就为本、由同行评议主导的研究体系。

sents.en: They could cement the status quo of peer-reviewed research.
sents.cn: 它们可能固化同行评议研究的现状。

sents.en: They do not fund peer-reviewed research.
sents.cn: 它们并不资助同行评议的研究。

sents.en: They perpetuate the myth of the lone genius.
sents.cn: 它们使“孤胆天才”的神话得以延续。

sents.en: The goals of the prize-givers seem as scattered as the criticism.
sents.cn: 颁奖者的目标似乎和批评意见一样五花八门。

sents.en: Some want to shock, others to draw people into science, or to better reward those who have made their careers in research.
sents.cn: 有人想要语惊四座，有人想把人们吸引到科学中来，还有人想更好地奖励那些以科研为业的人。

sents.en: As Nature has pointed out before, there are some legitimate concerns about how science prizes—both new and old—are distributed.
sents.cn: 正如《自然》杂志此前所指出的，对于科学奖项——无论新旧——如何颁授，确实存在一些合理的担忧。

sents.en: The Breakthrough Prize in Life Sciences, launched this year, takes an unrepresentative view of what the life sciences include.
sents.cn: 今年设立的“生命科学突破奖”，对生命科学所涵盖的范围持有一种以偏概全的看法。

sents.en: But the Nobel Foundation's limit of three recipients per prize, each of whom must still be living, has long been outgrown by the collaborative nature of modern research—as will be demonstrated by the inevitable row over who is ignored when it comes to acknowledging the discovery of the Higgs boson.
sents.cn: 但诺贝尔基金会关于每个奖项至多三名得主、且每位得主都必须在世的限制，早已不能适应现代科研的协作性质——当人们要为希格斯玻色子的发现论功行赏时，围绕谁被忽略而必然爆发的争论便会证明这一点。

sents.en: The Nobels were, of course, themselves set up by a very rich individual who had decided what he wanted to do with his own money.
sents.cn: 当然，诺贝尔奖本身也是由一位非常富有的人设立的，是他决定了要拿自己的钱来做什么。

sents.en: Time, rather than intention, has given them legitimacy.
sents.cn: 赋予这些奖项以正当性的，是时间，而非设立者的初衷。

sents.en: As much as some scientists may complain about the new awards, two things seem clear.
sents.cn: 尽管一些科学家或许会对这些新奖项颇有微词，但有两点似乎是明确的。

sents.en: First, most researchers would accept such a prize if they were offered one.
sents.cn: 第一，倘若有人提供这样一个奖项，大多数研究者都会接受。

sents.en: Second, it is surely a good thing that the money and attention come to science rather than go elsewhere, It is fair to criticize and question the mechanism—that is the culture of research, after all—but it is the prize-givers' money to do with as they please.
sents.cn: 第二，让金钱和关注流向科学而非别处，无疑是一件好事。对其评奖机制加以批评和质疑是无可厚非的——毕竟这正是科研的文化——但这毕竟是颁奖者自己的钱，他们爱怎么用就怎么用。

sents.en: It is wise to take such gifts with gratitude and grace.
sents.cn: 明智之举是怀着感激与雅量接受这样的馈赠。

sents.en: "The Heart of the Matter," the just-released report by the American Academy of Arts and Sciences (AAAS), deserves praise for affirming the importance of the humanities and social sciences to the prosperity and security of liberal democracy in America.
sents.cn: 美国艺术与科学院刚刚发布的报告《问题的核心》，因肯定了人文学科与社会科学对美国自由民主制度之繁荣与安全的重要性而值得赞许。

sents.en: Regrettably, however, the report's failure to address the true nature of the crisis facing liberal education may cause more harm than good.
sents.cn: 然而遗憾的是，该报告未能触及自由教育所面临危机的真正本质，这或许会弊大于利。

sents.en: In 2010, leading congressional Democrats and Republicans sent letters to the AAAS asking that it identify actions that could be taken by "federal, state and local governments, universities, foundations, educators, individual benefactors and others" to "maintain national excellence in humanities and social scientific scholarship and education."In response, the American Academy formed the Commission on the Humanities and Social Sciences.
sents.cn: 2010年，国会民主党与共和党的领袖们致函美国艺术与科学院，请其指明“联邦、州及地方政府、大学、基金会、教育工作者、个人捐助者及其他各方”可以采取哪些行动，以“保持人文学科与社会科学学术研究和教育领域的国家卓越水平”。作为回应，美国艺术与科学院成立了人文与社会科学委员会。

sents.en: Among the commission's 51 members are top-tier-university presidents, scholars, lawyers, judges, and business executives, as well as prominent figures from diplomacy, filmmaking, music and journalism.
sents.cn: 该委员会的51名成员中，既有一流大学的校长、学者、律师、法官和企业高管，也有来自外交、电影制作、音乐和新闻界的知名人士。

sents.en: The goals identified in the report are generally admirable.
sents.cn: 报告中提出的目标总体上令人钦佩。

sents.en: Because representative government presupposes an informed citizenry, the report supports full literacy; stresses the study of history and government, particularly American history and American government; and encourages the use of new digital technologies.
sents.cn: 由于代议制政府以公民知情为前提，该报告主张实现全民充分的读写能力；强调对历史和政治、尤其是美国历史和美国政治的研究；并鼓励运用新的数字技术。

sents.en: To encourage innovation and competition, the report calls for increased investment in research, the crafting of coherent curricula that improve students' ability to solve problems and communicate effectively in the 21st century, increased funding for teachers and the encouragement of scholars to bring their learning to bear on the great challenges of the day.
sents.cn: 为鼓励创新与竞争，该报告呼吁加大科研投入，精心设计连贯的课程以提升学生在21世纪解决问题和有效沟通的能力，增加对教师的经费投入，并鼓励学者运用所学来应对当今的重大挑战。

sents.en: The report also advocates greater study of foreign languages, international affairs and the expansion of study abroad programs.
sents.cn: 该报告还提倡加强对外语和国际事务的学习，并扩大海外留学项目。

sents.en: Unfortunately, despite 2½ years in the making, "The Heart of the Matter" never gets to the heart of the matter: the illiberal nature of liberal education at our leading colleges and universities.
sents.cn: 遗憾的是，尽管历时两年半才完成，《问题的核心》却始终未能触及问题的核心：我国一流高校自由教育所具有的反自由本质。

sents.en: The commission ignores that for several decades America's colleges and universities have produced graduates who don't know the content and character of liberal education and are thus deprived of its benefits.
sents.cn: 该委员会无视这样一个事实：几十年来，美国的高校培养出的毕业生并不了解自由教育的内涵与特质，因而也被剥夺了自由教育所能带来的益处。

sents.en: Sadly, the spirit of inquiry once at home on campus has been replaced by the use of the humanities and social sciences as vehicles for publicizing "progressive," or left-liberal propaganda.
sents.cn: 可悲的是，曾经在校园里蔚然成风的探究精神，已被人文学科和社会科学所取代——它们沦为宣扬“进步主义”即左翼自由主义思想的工具。
