sents.en: Today, professors routinely treat the progressive interpretation of history and progressive public policy as the proper subject of study while portraying conservative or classical liberal ideas—such as free markets or self-reliance —as falling outside the boundaries of routine, and sometimes legitimate, intellectual investigation.
sents.cn: 如今，教授们惯于把对历史的进步主义解读和进步主义的公共政策当作恰当的研究课题，却把保守主义或古典自由主义的理念——如自由市场或自力更生——描绘成超出常规、有时甚至超出正当智识探究范围之外的东西。

sents.en: The AAAS displays great enthusiasm for liberal education.
sents.cn: 美国艺术与科学院对自由教育表现出极大的热情。

sents.en: Yet its report may well set back reform by obscuring the depth and breadth of the challenge that Congress asked it to illuminate.
sents.cn: 然而，其报告掩盖了国会请其阐明的这一挑战在深度和广度上的真相，很可能反而会使改革倒退。

sents.en: King Juan Carlos of Spain once insisted "kings don't abdicate, they die in their sleep."
sents.cn: 西班牙国王胡安·卡洛斯曾坚称，“国王不会退位，他们会在睡梦中死去”。

sents.en: But embarrassing scandals and the popularity of the republican left in the recent Euro-elections have forced him to eat his words and stand down.
sents.cn: 但是，一连串令人难堪的丑闻，以及共和派左翼在最近欧洲议会选举中人气高涨，迫使他收回前言、黯然退位。

sents.en: So, does the Spanish crisis suggest that monarchy is seeing its last days?
sents.cn: 那么，西班牙的这场危机是否意味着君主制正走向终结？

sents.en: Does that mean the writing is on the wall for all European royals, with their magnificent uniforms and majestic lifestyles?
sents.cn: 这是否意味着，对所有身着华美制服、过着尊贵生活的欧洲王室成员而言，覆灭的征兆已然显现？

sents.en: The Spanish case provides arguments both for and against monarchy.
sents.cn: 西班牙的例子为君主制提供了支持与反对两方面的论据。

sents.en: When public opinion is particularly polarised, as it was following the end of the Franco regime, monarchs can rise above "mere"politics and "embody" a spirit of national unity.
sents.cn: 当公众舆论严重两极分化时，正如佛朗哥政权终结后的情形那样，君主能够超越“纯粹的”政治，“体现”民族团结的精神。

sents.en: It is this apparent transcendence of politics that explains monarchs' continuing popularity as heads of state.
sents.cn: 正是这种对政治的明显超越，解释了君主作为国家元首何以长盛不衰、深得人心。

sents.en: And so, the Middle East excepted, Europe is the most monarch-infested region in the world, with 10 kingdoms (not counting Vatican City and Andorra).
sents.cn: 因此，除中东地区之外，欧洲是世界上君主最为密集的地区，拥有10个王国（梵蒂冈城国和安道尔尚未计入）。

sents.en: But unlike their absolutist counterparts in the Gulf and Asia, most royal families have survived because they allow voters to avoid the difficult search for a non-controversial but respected public figure.
sents.cn: 但与海湾地区和亚洲那些专制的同类不同，大多数王室之所以得以延续，是因为它们让选民免去了寻觅一位无争议却又受人尊敬的公众人物这一难题。

sents.en: Even so, kings and queens undoubtedly have a downside.
sents.cn: 即便如此，国王和王后无疑也有其弊端。

sents.en: Symbolic of national unity as they claim to be, their very history—and sometimes the way they behave today—embodies outdated and indefensible privileges and inequalities.
sents.cn: 尽管他们自诩为民族团结的象征，但他们的历史本身——有时还有他们今日的行为方式——所体现的，却是过时且站不住脚的特权与不平等。

sents.en: At a time when Thomas Piketty and other economists are warning of rising inequality and the increasing power of inherited wealth, it is bizarre that wealthy aristocratic families should still be the symbolic heart of modern democratic states.
sents.cn: 当托马斯·皮凯蒂等经济学家正告诫人们不平等日益加剧、继承财富的势力不断膨胀之时，富有的贵族家庭竟仍是现代民主国家象征意义上的核心，这实在是咄咄怪事。

sents.en: The most successful monarchies strive to abandon or hide their old aristocratic ways.
sents.cn: 最成功的君主制都竭力摒弃或掩饰其陈旧的贵族作风。

sents.en: Princes and princesses have day-jobs and ride bicycles, not horses (or helicopters).
sents.cn: 王子和公主们都有日常工作，骑的是自行车，而非骏马（或直升机）。

sents.en: Even so, these are wealthy families who party with the international 1%, and media intrusiveness makes it increasingly difficult to maintain the right image.
sents.cn: 即便如此，他们仍是与国际上那1%的富人一同寻欢作乐的富裕家庭，而媒体的无孔不入使得维持得体的形象愈发困难。

sents.en: While Europe's monarchies will no doubt be smart enough to survive for some time to come, it is the British royals who have most to fear from the Spanish example.
sents.cn: 尽管欧洲各君主制无疑会足够精明，得以在未来一段时间内继续存续，但最该从西班牙这一例子中感到恐惧的，却是英国王室。

sents.en: It is only the Queen who has preserved the monarchy's reputation with her rather ordinary (if well-heeled) granny style.
sents.cn: 唯有女王凭借其相当寻常（虽说颇为优渥）的祖母般作风，才得以维护君主制的声誉。

sents.en: The danger will come with Charles, who has both an expensive taste of lifestyle and a pretty hierarchical view of the world.
sents.cn: 危险将随查尔斯而来，他既有奢华的生活品味，又抱有相当等级森严的世界观。

sents.en: He has failed to understand that monarchies have largely survived because they provide a service—as non-controversial and non-political heads of state.
sents.cn: 他未能理解，君主制之所以大体得以存续，是因为它们提供了一种服务——充当无争议、超党派的国家元首。

sents.en: Charles ought to know that as English history shows, it is kings, not republicans, who are the monarchy's worst enemies.
sents.cn: 查尔斯本应明白，正如英国历史所表明的那样，君主制最大的敌人是国王，而非共和派。

sents.en: Just how much does the Constitution protect your digital data?
sents.cn: 宪法究竟能在多大程度上保护你的数字数据？

sents.en: The Supreme Court will now consider whether police can search the contents of a mobile phone without a warrant if the phone is on or around a person during an arrest.
sents.cn: 最高法院如今将审议这样一个问题：在逮捕过程中，如果手机就在被捕者身上或其周围，警方能否在未获搜查令的情况下搜查手机内容。

sents.en: California has asked the justices to refrain from a sweeping ruling, particularly one that upsets the old assumption that authorities may search through the possessions of suspects at the time of their arrest.
sents.cn: 加利福尼亚州已请求大法官们不要作出大范围的裁决，尤其是那种会颠覆旧有假定的裁决——根据该假定，当局可以在逮捕嫌疑人时搜查其随身物品。

sents.en: It is hard, the state argues, for judges to assess the implications of new and rapidly changing technologies.
sents.cn: 该州辩称，法官很难评估新兴且日新月异的技术所带来的影响。

sents.en: The court would be recklessly modest if it followed California's advice.
sents.cn: 倘若法院听从加州的建议，那将是一种鲁莽的谦逊。

sents.en: Enough of the implications are discernable, even obvious, so that the justices can and should provide updated guidelines to police,lawyers and defendants.
sents.cn: 其中足够多的影响是可以辨识的，甚至是显而易见的，因此大法官们能够、也应当为警方、律师和被告提供更新后的指导原则。

sents.en: They should start by discarding California's lame argument that exploring the contents of a smartphone — a vast storehouse of digital information — is similar to, say, going through a suspect's purse.
sents.cn: 他们首先应当摒弃加州那个站不住脚的论点——即查看智能手机（一个庞大的数字信息库）的内容，与诸如翻查嫌疑人钱包之类的行为相似。

sents.en: The court has ruled that police don't violate the Fourth Amendment when they go through the wallet or pocketbook of an arrestee without a warrant.
sents.cn: 法院曾裁定，警方在未获搜查令的情况下翻查被捕者的钱夹或钱包，并不违反宪法第四修正案。

sents.en: But exploring one's smartphone is more like entering his or her home.
sents.cn: 但查看一个人的智能手机，则更像是进入了他或她的家。

sents.en: A smartphone may contain an arrestee's reading history, financial history, medical history and comprehensive records of recent correspondence.
sents.cn: 一部智能手机可能包含被捕者的阅读记录、财务记录、就医记录，以及近期通信的完整记载。

sents.en: The development of "cloud computing," meanwhile, has made that exploration so much the easier.
sents.cn: 与此同时，“云计算”的发展使得这种查看变得容易得多。

sents.en: Americans should take steps to protect their digital privacy.
sents.cn: 美国人应当采取措施保护自己的数字隐私。

sents.en: But keeping sensitive information on these devices is increasingly a requirement of normal life.
sents.cn: 但在这些设备上保存敏感信息，正日益成为正常生活的一种必需。

sents.en: Citizens still have a right to expect private documents to remain private and protected by the Constitution's prohibition on unreasonable searches.
sents.cn: 公民仍然有权期望私人文件保持私密，并受到宪法禁止不合理搜查这一条款的保护。

sents.en: As so often is the case, stating that principle doesn't ease the challenge of line-drawing.
sents.cn: 正如常常发生的那样，阐明这一原则并不能减轻划定界限的难度。

sents.en: In many cases, it would not be overly burdensome for authorities to obtain a warrant to search through phone contents.
sents.cn: 在许多情况下，当局为搜查手机内容而申请一张搜查令，并不会构成过重的负担。

sents.en: They could still invalidate Fourth Amendment protections when facing severe,urgent circumstances, and they could take reasonable measures to ensure that phone data are not erased or altered while waiting for a warrant.
sents.cn: 在面临严峻、紧急的情况时，他们仍可使第四修正案的保护失效；而且在等待搜查令期间，他们也可以采取合理措施，确保手机数据不被删除或篡改。

sents.en: The court, though, may want to allow room for police to cite situations where they are entitled to more freedom.
sents.cn: 不过，法院或许会希望留有余地，让警方得以援引那些他们有权享有更多自由的情形。

sents.en: But the justices should not swallow California's argument whole.
sents.cn: 但大法官们不应全盘接受加州的论点。

sents.en: New, disruptive technology sometimes demands novel applications of the Constitution's protections.
sents.cn: 新兴的、具有颠覆性的技术，有时要求人们以新颖的方式来适用宪法的各项保护。

sents.en: Orin Kerr, a law professor, compares the explosion and accessibility of digital information in the 21st century with the establishment of automobile use as a virtual necessity of life in the 20th: The justices had to specify novel rules for the new personal domain of the passenger car then; they must sort out how the Fourth Amendment applies to digital information now.
sents.cn: 法学教授奥林·克尔将21世纪数字信息的爆炸式增长及其易得性，与20世纪汽车的使用确立为生活几乎不可或缺之必需这一过程相类比：当年，大法官们不得不为乘用车这一新的私人领域制定新颖的规则；如今，他们则必须厘清第四修正案该如何适用于数字信息。

sents.en: The journal Science is adding an extra round of statistical checks to its peer-review process, editor-in-chief Marcia McNutt announced today.
sents.cn: 《科学》杂志正在其同行评议流程中增加一道额外的统计学审查，该杂志主编玛西娅·麦克纳特于今日宣布了这一消息。

sents.en: The policy follows similar efforts from other journals, after widespread concern that basic mistakes in data analysis are contributing to the irreproducibility of many published research findings.
sents.cn: 在人们普遍担忧数据分析中的基本错误正导致众多已发表研究结论无法重现之后，这一举措效仿了其他期刊的类似做法。

sents.en: "Readers must have confidence in the conclusions published in our journal," writes McNutt in an editorial.
sents.cn: 麦克纳特在一篇社论中写道：“读者必须对本刊所发表结论抱有信心。”

sents.en: Working with the American Statistical Association, the journal has appointed seven experts to a statistic board of reviewing editors (SBoRE).
sents.cn: 该杂志与美国统计协会合作，任命了七名专家组成统计审稿编辑委员会（SBoRE）。

sents.en: Manuscript will be flagged up for additional scrutiny by the journal's internal editors, or by its existing Board of Reviewing Editors or by outside peer reviewers.
sents.cn: 稿件将由杂志内部编辑、现有的审稿编辑委员会或外部同行评审员标记出来，以接受额外的审查。

sents.en: The SBoRE panel will then find external statisticians to review these manuscripts.
sents.cn: 随后，SBoRE专家组将物色外部统计学家来评审这些稿件。

sents.en: Asked whether any particular papers had impelled the change, McNutt said: "The creation of the 'statistics board' was motivated by concerns broadly with the application of statistics and data analysis in scientific research and is part of Science's overall drive to increase reproducibility in the research we publish."
sents.cn: 当被问及是否有某些具体论文促成了这一变化时，麦克纳特表示：“设立‘统计委员会’，其动因在于对科学研究中统计学与数据分析之应用的广泛担忧，并且这是《科学》杂志为提高所发表研究之可重现性而做出的整体努力的一部分。”

sents.en: Giovanni Parmigiani, a biostatistician at the Harvard School of Public Health, a member of the SBoRE group, says he expects the board to "play primarily an advisory role."
sents.cn: 乔瓦尼·帕尔米贾尼是哈佛大学公共卫生学院的一位生物统计学家，也是SBoRE小组的成员，他表示自己预计该委员会将“主要发挥咨询作用”。

sents.en: He agreed to join because he "found the foresight behind the establishment of the SBoRE to be novel, unique and likely to have a lasting impact. This impact will not only be through the publications in Science itself, but hopefully through a larger group of publishing places that may want to model their approach after Science."
sents.cn: 他之所以同意加入，是因为他“觉得设立SBoRE背后的这份远见新颖、独到，且很可能产生持久的影响。这种影响不仅将通过《科学》杂志自身的出版物得以实现，而且有望波及更广泛的一批出版机构——它们或许会希望仿效《科学》的做法”。

sents.en: John Ioannidis, a physician who studies research methodology, says that the policy is "a most welcome step forward" and "long overdue." "Most journals are weak in statistical review, and this damages the quality of what they publish. I think that, for the majority of scientific papers nowadays, statistical review is more essential than expert review," he says.
sents.cn: 约翰·约安尼迪斯是一位研究科研方法论的医生，他表示这一举措是“一个极受欢迎的进步”，而且“早就该如此了”。“大多数期刊在统计学审查方面都很薄弱，这损害了它们所发表内容的质量。我认为，对当今绝大多数科学论文而言，统计学审查比专家审查更为关键，”他说。

sents.en: But he noted that biomedical journals such as Annals of Internal Medicine, the Journal of the American Medical Association and The Lancet pay strong attention to statistical review.
sents.cn: 但他指出，《内科学年鉴》《美国医学会杂志》和《柳叶刀》等生物医学期刊，对统计学审查给予了高度重视。

sents.en: Professional scientists are expected to know how to analyze data, but statistical errors are alarmingly common in published research, according to David Vaux, a cell biologist.
sents.cn: 细胞生物学家戴维·沃克斯认为，人们期望专业科学家懂得如何分析数据，但已发表研究中的统计学错误却普遍得令人担忧。

sents.en: Researchers should improve their standards, he wrote in 2012, but journals should also take a tougher line, "engaging reviewers who are statistically literate and editors who can verify the process."
sents.cn: 他在2012年写道，研究人员应当提高自身的标准，但期刊也应采取更为严厉的立场，“聘用具备统计学素养的评审员，以及能够核查整个流程的编辑”。

sents.en: Vaux says that Science's idea to pass some papers to statisticians "has some merit, but a weakness is that it relies on the board of reviewing editors to identify 'the papers that need scrutiny' in the first place".
sents.cn: 沃克斯表示，《科学》杂志将部分论文交由统计学家审阅的设想“有一定可取之处，但其弱点在于，它一开始就依赖审稿编辑委员会去甄别‘那些需要审查的论文’”。

sents.en: Two years ago, Rupert Murdoch's daughter, Elisabeth, spoke of the "unsettling dearth of integrity across so many of our institutions."
sents.cn: 两年前，鲁伯特·默多克的女儿伊丽莎白曾谈到“我们如此众多的机构中普遍存在的令人不安的诚信缺失”。

sents.en: Integrity had collapsed, she argued, because of a collective acceptance that the only "sorting mechanism" in society should be profit and the market.
sents.cn: 她认为，诚信之所以崩塌，是因为人们集体认同社会中唯一的“筛选机制”理应是利润与市场。

sents.en: But "it's us, human beings, we the people who create the society we want, not profit."
sents.cn: 但“创造我们所向往之社会的，是我们，是人类，是我们普通民众，而不是利润”。

sents.en: Driving her point home, she continued: "It's increasingly apparent that the absence of purpose, of a moral language within government, media or business could become one of the most dangerous goals for capitalism and freedom."
sents.cn: 为了把观点说透，她接着说道：“越来越明显的是，政府、媒体或商界中目标的缺失、道德语言的缺失，可能会成为资本主义与自由所面临的最危险的目标之一。”

sents.en: This same absence of moral purpose was wounding companies such as News International, she thought, making it more likely that it would lose its way as it had with widespread illegal telephone hacking.
sents.cn: 她认为，正是这种道德目标的缺失，正在损害诸如新闻国际公司之类的企业，使其更有可能误入歧途——正如该公司在大规模非法电话窃听一事上所表现的那样。

sents.en: As the hacking trial concludes—finding guilty one ex-editor of the News of the World, Andy Coulson, for conspiring to hack phones, and finding his predecessor, Rebekah Brooks, innocent of the same charge —the wider issue of dearth of integrity still stands.Journalists are known to have hacked the phones of up to 5,500 people.
sents.cn: 随着窃听案审判落下帷幕——《世界新闻报》前主编安迪·库尔森因密谋窃听电话被判有罪，而其前任丽贝卡·布鲁克斯则被判定不构成同一指控——诚信缺失这一更广泛的问题依然存在。据悉，记者们窃听过多达5500人的电话。

sents.en: This is hacking on an industrial scale, as was acknowledged by Glenn Mulcaire, the man hired by the News of the World in 2001 to be the point person for phone hacking.
sents.cn: 这是工业化规模的窃听，正如格伦·穆尔凯尔所承认的那样——他是《世界新闻报》于2001年雇来专门负责电话窃听的人。

sents.en: Others await trial.
sents.cn: 另有一些人正等待受审。

sents.en: This long story still unfolds.
sents.cn: 这个漫长的故事仍在继续上演。

sents.en: In many respects, the dearth of moral purpose frames not only the fact of such widespread phone hacking but the terms on which the trial took place.
sents.cn: 在许多方面，道德目标的缺失不仅勾勒出如此大规模电话窃听这一事实，也勾勒出此次审判得以进行所依据的种种条件。

sents.en: One of the astonishing revelations was how little Rebekah Brooks knew of what went on in her newsroom, how little she thought to ask and the fact that she never inquired how the stories arrived.
sents.cn: 其中一个令人震惊的事实披露是：丽贝卡·布鲁克斯对自己编辑部里所发生的事情竟知之甚少，她几乎没想过要去过问，而且她从不追问那些报道是如何得来的。

sents.en: The core of her successful defence was that she knew nothing.
sents.cn: 她成功辩护的核心，便是她对此一无所知。

sents.en: In today's world, it has become normal that well-paid executives should not be accountable for what happens in the organizations that they run.
sents.cn: 在当今世界，薪酬优厚的高管对其所掌管机构内发生之事不必负责，已经成了一件稀松平常的事。

sents.en: Perhaps we should not be so surprised.
sents.cn: 或许我们本不该如此惊讶。

sents.en: For a generation, the collective doctrine has been that the sorting mechanism of society should be profit.
sents.cn: 一代人以来，集体信奉的教条一直是：社会的筛选机制理应是利润。

sents.en: The words that have mattered are efficiency, flexibility, shareholder value, business–friendly, wealth generation, sales, impact and, in newspapers, circulation.
sents.cn: 受到看重的词汇，是效率、灵活、股东价值、利商、财富创造、销售额、影响力，而在报业则是发行量。

sents.en: Words degraded to the margin have been justice, fairness, tolerance, proportionality and accountability.
sents.cn: 而被贬至边缘的词汇，则是正义、公平、宽容、相称性与问责。

sents.en: The purpose of editing the News of the World was not to promote reader understanding, to be fair in what was written or to betray any common humanity.
sents.cn: 主编《世界新闻报》的目的，既不是增进读者的理解，也不是在所写内容上做到公正，更不是体现任何共通的人性。

sents.en: It was to ruin lives in the quest for circulation and impact.
sents.cn: 其目的在于，为追逐发行量与影响力而毁掉他人的生活。

sents.en: Ms Brooks may or may not have had suspicions about how her journalists got their stories, but she asked no questions, gave no instructions—nor received traceable, recorded answers.
sents.cn: 布鲁克斯女士对她手下记者如何获取报道也许有所怀疑，也许并无怀疑，但她既不发问，也不下达指令——更没有收到任何有据可查、有案可稽的答复。

sents.en: France, which prides itself as the global innovator of fashion, has decided its fashion industry has lost an absolute right to define physical beauty for woman.
sents.cn: 法国以自身作为全球时尚潮流的引领者而自豪，但它如今认定，本国的时尚业已不再拥有为女性界定身材之美的绝对权力。

sents.en: Its lawmakers gave preliminary approval last week to a law that would make it a crime to employ ultra-thin models on runways.
sents.cn: 上周，法国立法者初步通过了一项法律：在T台上雇用过度纤瘦的模特将被定为犯罪。

sents.en: The parliament also agreed to ban websites that"incite excessive thinness" by promoting extreme dieting.
sents.cn: 议会还同意封禁那些通过鼓吹极端节食而“怂恿人们过度追求纤瘦”的网站。

sents.en: Such measures have a couple of uplifting motives.
sents.cn: 这类举措蕴含着若干令人振奋的动机。

sents.en: They suggest beauty should not be defined by looks that end up impinging on health.
sents.cn: 它们传递出这样的理念：美不应由那些最终危害健康的外貌来界定。

sents.en: That's a start.
sents.cn: 这是一个开端。

sents.en: And the ban on ultra-thin models seems to go beyond protecting models from starving themselves to death –as some have done.
sents.cn: 而禁用过度纤瘦的模特，其意义似乎不止于保护模特免于因节食而把自己饿死——已有一些模特如此丧命。

sents.en: It tells the fashion industry that it must take responsibility for the signal it sends women, especially teenage girls, about the social tape –measure they must use to determine their individual worth.
sents.cn: 它告诫时尚业：对于它向女性、尤其是少女传递的那把用以衡量自身价值的“社会标尺”所发出的信号，时尚业必须承担起责任。

sents.en: The bans, if fully enforced, would suggest to woman (and many men)that they should not let others be arbiters of their beauty.
sents.cn: 这些禁令若能得到全面落实，便是在提醒女性（以及许多男性）：她们不应让他人来充当评判自身之美的裁判。

sents.en: And perhaps faintly, they hint that people should look to intangible qualities like character and intellect rather than dieting their way to size zero or wasp-waist physiques.
sents.cn: 而且，它们或许还隐约暗示，人们应当看重品格与才智这类无形的品质，而不是靠节食把自己饿成“零号身材”或“蜂腰”体型。

sents.en: The French measures, however, rely too much on severe punishment to change a culture that still regards beauty as skin-deep-and bone-showing.
sents.cn: 然而，法国的这些举措过于依赖严厉的惩罚，去改变一种仍把美看作肤浅外表、看作骨感外露的文化。

sents.en: Under the law, using a fashion model that does not meet a government-defined index of body mass could result in a $85, 000 fine and six months in prison.
sents.cn: 根据该法律，若启用身体质量指数不符合政府规定标准的时装模特，可被处以八万五千美元罚款并监禁六个月。

sents.en: The fashion industry knows it has an inherent problem in focusing on material adornment and idealized body types.
sents.cn: 时尚业清楚，在专注于物质装扮和理想化身材这一点上，它存在着固有的问题。

sents.en: In Denmark, the United States, and a few other countries, it is trying to set voluntary standard for models and fashion images that rely more on peer pressure for enforcement.
sents.cn: 在丹麦、美国以及其他几个国家，时尚业正尝试为模特和时尚形象设立自愿性标准，更多地依靠同行压力来推动落实。

sents.en: In contrast to France's actions, Denmark's fashion industry agreed last month on rules and sanctions regarding the age, health, and other characteristics of models.
sents.cn: 与法国的做法形成对比，丹麦时尚业上个月就模特的年龄、健康及其他特征方面的规则与处罚达成了一致。

sents.en: The newly revised Danish Fashion Ethical Charter clearly states: "We are aware of and take responsibility for the impact the fashion industry has on body ideals, especially on young people. " The charter's main tool of enforcement is to deny access for designers and modeling agencies to Copenhagen.
sents.cn: 新修订的《丹麦时尚伦理宪章》明确宣告：“我们意识到并愿意承担起时尚业对身材审美、尤其是对年轻人所产生的影响之责任。”该宪章主要的执行手段，是拒绝让设计师和模特经纪公司进入哥本哈根。

sents.en: Fashion Week, which is run by the Danish Fashion Institute.
sents.cn: （哥本哈根）时装周由丹麦时尚学院主办。

sents.en: But in general it relies on a name-and –shame method of compliance.
sents.cn: 但总体而言，它依靠的是一种“点名羞辱”的方式来促使各方遵守。

sents.en: Relying on ethical persuasion rather than law to address the misuse of body ideals may be the best step.
sents.cn: 依靠道德劝导而非法律来解决滥用理想身材这一问题，或许是最佳之策。

sents.en: Even better would be to help elevate notions of beauty beyond the material standards of a particular industry.
sents.cn: 而更好的做法，是帮助人们把美的观念提升到超越某一特定行业物质标准的高度。

sents.en: For the first time in the history more people live in towns than in the country.
sents.cn: 历史上第一次，居住在城镇的人口超过了居住在乡村的人口。

sents.en: In Britain this has had a curious result.
sents.cn: 在英国，这带来了一个耐人寻味的结果。

sents.en: While polls show Britons rate "the countryside" alongside the royal family.
sents.cn: 民意调查显示，英国人把“乡村”与王室、

sents.en: Shakespeare and the National Health Service (NHS) as what makes them proudest of their country, this has limited political support.
sents.cn: 莎士比亚以及国民医疗服务体系（NHS）相提并论，视其为最令他们为自己国家感到自豪的事物，然而乡村所获得的政治支持却很有限。

sents.en: A century ago Octavia Hill launched the National Trust not to rescue stylish houses but to save "the beauty of natural places for everyone forever".
sents.cn: 一个世纪前，奥克塔维娅·希尔创立了国民信托组织，其宗旨并非拯救华美的宅邸，而是为了“永远为每一个人守护自然之地的美”。

sents.en: It was specifically to provide city dwellers with spaces for leisure where they could experience "a refreshing air".
sents.cn: 其目的尤其在于为城市居民提供休闲的空间，让他们能够在那里享受“清新的空气”。

sents.en: Hill's pressure later led to the creation of national parks and green belts.
sents.cn: 希尔的推动后来促成了国家公园和绿化带的设立。

sents.en: They don't make countryside any more, and every year concrete consumes more of it.
sents.cn: 乡村不会再被造出来，而每年都有越来越多的乡村被混凝土吞噬。

sents.en: It needs constant guardianship.
sents.cn: 它需要持续不断的守护。

sents.en: At the next election none of the big parties seem likely to endorse this sentiment.
sents.cn: 在下一次大选中，几大政党似乎都不太可能认同这一理念。

sents.en: The Conservatives' planning reform explicitly gives rural development priority over conservation, even authorizing "off–plan" building where local people might object.
sents.cn: 保守党的规划改革明确把乡村开发置于保护之上，甚至在当地民众可能反对的地方也批准“未列入规划”的建设。

sents.en: The concept of sustainable development has been defined as profitable.
sents.cn: 可持续发展的概念已被界定为“有利可图”。

sents.en: Labour likewise wants to discontinue local planning where councils oppose development.
sents.cn: 工党同样主张，在地方议会反对开发之处，停止地方规划。

sents.en: The Liberal Democrats are silent Only Ukip, sensing its chance, has sides with those pleading for a more considered approach to using green land.
sents.cn: 自由民主党则保持沉默；只有英国独立党（Ukip）嗅到了自己的机会，站到了那些恳请以更审慎的方式利用绿地的人们一边。

sents.en: Its Campaign to Protect Rural England struck terror into many local Consecutive parties.
sents.cn: 其“保护英格兰乡村运动”令许多地方的保守党组织大为恐慌。

sents.en: The sensible place to build new houses, factories and offices is where people are, in cities and towns where infrastructure is in place.
sents.cn: 建造新住宅、工厂和办公楼的明智之地，应当是人口所在之处，即基础设施已经到位的城市与城镇。

sents.en: The London agents Stirling Ackroyd recently identified enough sites for half a million houses in the London area alone, with no intrusion on green belts.
sents.cn: 伦敦的地产代理公司斯特林·阿克罗伊德（Stirling Ackroyd）近期发现，仅在伦敦地区就有足够的地块可建五十万套住宅，而无须侵占绿化带。

sents.en: What is true of London is even truer of the provinces.
sents.cn: 对伦敦适用的情况，对外省地区而言更是如此。

sents.en: The idea that "housing crisis" equals "concreted meadows" is pure lobby talk.
sents.cn: 认为“住房危机”就等同于“把草地浇筑成混凝土”，这纯粹是游说集团的说辞。

sents.en: The issue is not the need for more houses but, as always, where to put them.
sents.cn: 问题不在于是否需要建造更多住房，而一如既往地在于把它们建在何处。

sents.en: Under lobby pressure, George Osborne favours rural new-build against urban renovation and renewal.
sents.cn: 在游说压力之下，乔治·奥斯本偏袒乡村新建，而不支持城市的改造与更新。

sents.en: He favours out-of-town shopping sites against high streets.
sents.cn: 他偏袒城外的购物场所，而不支持城内的商业街。

sents.en: This is not a free market but a biased one.
sents.cn: 这并非自由市场，而是一个有所偏向的市场。

sents.en: Rural towns and villages have grown and will always grow.
sents.cn: 乡村的城镇与村庄过去一直在扩展，将来也会一直扩展下去。

sents.en: They do so best where building sticks to their edges and respects their character.
sents.cn: 当建设贴着它们的边缘进行、并尊重它们的特色时，这种扩展最为理想。

sents.en: We do not ruin urban conservation areas.
sents.cn: 我们不会去毁掉城市里的保护区。

sents.en: Why ruin rural ones?
sents.cn: 那又何必去毁掉乡村的保护区呢？

sents.en: Development should be planned, not let trip, After the Netherlands, Britain is Europe's most crowded country.
sents.cn: 开发应当有所规划，而不应放任自流。继荷兰之后，英国是欧洲人口最稠密的国家。

sents.en: Half a century of town and country planning has enabled it to retain an enviable rural coherence, while still permitting low-density urban living.
sents.cn: 半个世纪的城乡规划，使英国得以保持令人艳羡的乡村整体风貌，同时又仍然允许低密度的城市生活。

sents.en: There is no doubt of the alternative-the corrupted landscapes of southern Portugal, Spain or Ireland.
sents.cn: 其替代结果是毋庸置疑的——那便是葡萄牙南部、西班牙或爱尔兰那些遭到破坏的景观。

sents.en: Avoiding this rather than promoting it should unite the left and right of the political spectrum.
sents.cn: 避免出现这种局面、而非推动其发生，理应让政治光谱上的左翼与右翼达成一致。

sents.en: "There is one and only one social responsibility of business"
sents.cn: “企业有且只有一项社会责任”，

sents.en: Wrote Milton Friedman, a Nobel Prize-winning economist "That is, to use its resources and engage in activities designed to increase its profits. " But even if you accept Friedman's premise and regard corporate social responsibility(CSR) policies as a waste of shareholders's money, things may not be absolutely clear-act.
sents.cn: 诺贝尔经济学奖得主米尔顿·弗里德曼如是写道，“那就是运用其资源、从事旨在增加自身利润的各种活动。”但是，即便你接受弗里德曼的这一前提，把企业社会责任（CSR）政策视为对股东资金的浪费，事情也未必绝对那么简单明了。

sents.en: New research suggests that CSR may create monetary value for companies at least when they are prosecuted for corruption.
sents.cn: 新的研究表明，企业社会责任或许能为企业创造货币价值——至少在企业因腐败而受到起诉时是如此。

sents.en: The largest firms in America and Britain together spend more than $15 billion a year on CSR, according to an estimate by EPG, a consulting firm.
sents.cn: 据咨询公司EPG估计，美国和英国最大的那些公司每年在企业社会责任上的开支合计超过一百五十亿美元。

sents.en: This could add value to their businesses in three ways.
sents.cn: 这种开支可能通过三种途径为其业务增添价值。

sents.en: First, consumers may take CSR spending as a "signal" that a company's products are of high quality.
sents.cn: 第一，消费者可能把企业社会责任方面的开支视为一种“信号”，表明该公司的产品质量上乘。

sents.en: Second, customers may be willing to buy a company's products as an indirect may to donate to the good causes it helps.
sents.cn: 第二，顾客可能愿意购买某公司的产品，以此作为向该公司所资助的公益事业间接捐款的一种方式。

sents.en: And third, through a more diffuse "halo effect" whereby its good deeds earn it greater consideration from consumers and others.
sents.cn: 第三，则是通过一种更为弥散的“光环效应”——企业的善行使其赢得消费者及他人更多的青睐。

sents.en: Previous studies on CSR have had trouble differentiating these effects because consumers can be affected by all three.
sents.cn: 以往关于企业社会责任的研究一直难以区分这几种效应，因为消费者可能同时受到这三者的影响。

sents.en: A recent study attempts to separate them by looking at bribery prosecutions under American's Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA).
sents.cn: 最近的一项研究试图把它们区分开来，办法是考察依据美国《反海外腐败法》（FCPA）所进行的贿赂起诉。

sents.en: It argues that since prosecutors do not consume a company's products as part of their investigations, they could be influenced only by the halo effect.
sents.cn: 该研究认为，由于检察官在调查过程中并不消费企业的产品，因此他们只可能受到光环效应的影响。

sents.en: The study found that, among prosecuted firms, those with the most comprehensive CSR programmes tended to get more lenient penalties.
sents.cn: 研究发现，在受到起诉的公司中，那些拥有最全面的企业社会责任项目的公司，往往会得到较为宽大的处罚。

sents.en: Their analysis ruled out the possibility that it was firm's political influence, rather than their CSR stand, that accounted for the leniency: Companies that contributed more to political campaigns did not receive lower fines.
sents.cn: 他们的分析排除了如下可能性，即起诉得到从宽处理的原因是公司的政治影响力、而非其企业社会责任表现：对政治竞选捐款较多的公司，并未获得较低的罚款。

sents.en: In all, the study concludes that whereas prosecutors should only evaluate a case based on its merits, they do seem to be influenced by a company's record in CSR.
sents.cn: 总而言之，该研究得出结论：检察官本应仅依据案件本身的是非曲直来评判，但他们似乎确实受到了公司在企业社会责任方面记录的影响。

sents.en: "We estimate that either eliminating a substantial labour-rights concern, such as child labour, or increasing corporate giving by about 20% result in fines that generally are 40% lower than the typical punishment for bribing foreign officials, " says one researcher.
sents.cn: 一位研究者说：“据我们估算，无论是消除诸如使用童工这类严重的劳工权益问题，还是把企业捐赠增加约百分之二十，所带来的罚款一般都会比贿赂外国官员的典型处罚低百分之四十。”

sents.en: Researchers admit that their study does not answer the question of how much businesses ought to spend on CSR.
sents.cn: 研究者承认，他们的研究并未回答企业究竟应在企业社会责任上投入多少这一问题。

sents.en: Nor does it reveal how much companies are banking on the halo effect, rather than the other possible benefits, when they decide their do-gooding policies.
sents.cn: 它也未揭示，企业在决定其行善政策时，究竟在多大程度上指望的是光环效应，而非其他可能的好处。

sents.en: But at least they have demonstrated that when companies get into trouble with the law, evidence of good character can win them a less costly punishment.
sents.cn: 但他们至少已经证明：当企业触犯法律、陷入麻烦时，良好品行的证据能为它们赢得较轻的处罚。

sents.en: There will eventually come a day when The New York Times ceases to publish stories on newsprint.
sents.cn: 终有一天，《纽约时报》将不再用新闻纸来印行报道。

sents.en: Exactly when that day will be is a matter of debate.
sents.cn: 那一天究竟会是何时，则尚有争议。

sents.en: "Sometime in the future, " the paper's publisher said back in 2010.
sents.cn: “在未来的某个时候吧。”该报的出版人早在2010年就这样说过。

sents.en: Nostalgia for ink on paper and the rustle of pages aside, there's plenty of incentive to ditch print.
sents.cn: 撇开对纸上油墨和翻页沙沙声的怀旧之情不谈，放弃纸质印刷的理由有很多。

sents.en: The infrastructure required to make a physical newspaper – printing presses, delivery trucks – isn't just expensive; it's excessive at a time when online – only competitors don't have the same set of financial constraints.
sents.cn: 制作一份实体报纸所需的基础设施——印刷机、运送卡车——不仅成本高昂；在纯线上竞争对手并不受同样一套财务约束的当下，这种投入更是过度的。

sents.en: Readers are migrating away from print anyway.
sents.cn: 况且，读者本就在逐渐弃纸质而去。

sents.en: And though print ad sales still dwarf their online and mobile counterparts, revenue from print is still declining.
sents.cn: 而且，尽管纸质广告的销售额仍然远远超过其线上和移动端的同类，来自纸质印刷的收入却依旧在下滑。

sents.en: Overhead may be high and circulation lower, but rushing to eliminate its print edition would be a mistake, says BuzzFeed CEO Jonah Peretti.
sents.cn: BuzzFeed的首席执行官乔纳·佩雷蒂表示，间接成本或许居高不下、发行量或许日益走低，但仓促取消其纸质版将是一个错误。

sents.en: Peretti says the Times shouldn't waste time getting out of the print business, but only if they go about doing it the right way.
sents.cn: 佩雷蒂说，《纽约时报》不该把时间浪费在迟迟不退出纸质业务上——但前提是要以正确的方式去做这件事。

sents.en: "Figuring out a way to accelerate that transition would make sense for them, " he said, "but if you discontinue it, you're going to have your most loyal customers really upset with you. "
sents.cn: “想出一个办法来加速那一转型，对他们而言是明智之举，”他说，“但如果你直接把它停掉，就会让你最忠实的顾客对你大为不满。”

sents.en: Sometimes that's worth making a change anyway.
sents.cn: 有时候，即便如此，做出改变也是值得的。

sents.en: Peretti gives the example of Netflix discontinuing its DVD-mailing service to focus on streaming.
sents.cn: 佩雷蒂举了奈飞（Netflix）的例子——它停掉了DVD邮寄业务，转而专注于流媒体。

sents.en: "It was seen as blunder, " he said.
sents.cn: “当时这被看作一个大失误，”他说。

sents.en: The move turned out to be foresighted.
sents.cn: 结果这一举措被证明颇具远见。

sents.en: And if Peretti were in charge at the Times?
sents.cn: 那么，倘若让佩雷蒂来执掌《纽约时报》，他会怎么做呢？

sents.en: "I wouldn't pick a year to end print, " he said.
sents.cn: “我不会去挑定某一年来终结纸质版，”他说。

sents.en: "I would raise prices and make it into more of a legacy product. "
sents.cn: “我会提高价格，把它打造成一种更具传承意味的产品。”

sents.en: The most loyal customers would still get the product they favor, the idea goes, and they'd feel like they were helping sustain the quality of something they believe in.
sents.cn: 其设想是：最忠实的顾客依旧能得到他们所钟爱的产品，而且他们会觉得自己是在帮助维系某种他们所珍视之物的品质。

sents.en: "So if you're overpaying for print, you could feel like you were helping, "
sents.cn: “所以，如果你为纸质版多付了钱，你会觉得自己是在出一份力，”

sents.en: Peretti said.
sents.cn: 佩雷蒂说。

sents.en: "Then increase it at a higher rate each year and essentially try to generate additional revenue. "
sents.cn: “然后再逐年以更高的幅度提价，本质上就是设法创造额外的收入。”

sents.en: In other words, if you're going to make a print product, make it for the people who are already obsessed with it.
sents.cn: 换言之，如果你打算做一份纸质产品，那就把它做给那些早已对它痴迷的人。

sents.en: Which may be what the Times is doing already.
sents.cn: 而这或许正是《纽约时报》已经在做的事。

sents.en: Getting the print edition seven days a week costs nearly $500 a year – more than twice as much as a digital – only subscription.
sents.cn: 一周七天都订阅纸质版每年要花将近五百美元——是纯数字版订阅费用的两倍多。

sents.en: "It's a really hard thing to do and it's a tremendous luxury that BuzzFeed doesn't have a legacy business, "
sents.cn: “这是一件非常艰难的事，而BuzzFeed没有需要传承的旧有业务，这是一种莫大的幸运，”

sents.en: Peretti remarked.
sents.cn: 佩雷蒂评论道。

sents.en: "But we're going to have questions like that where we have things we're doing that don't make sense when the market changes and the world changes. In those situations, it's better to be more aggressive that less aggressive. "
sents.cn: “但我们今后也会遇到类似的问题：当市场和世界发生变化时，我们手头某些正在做的事情就变得不再合理。在这种情况下，激进一些总比保守一些要好。”

sents.en: First two hours, now three hours—this is how far in advance authorities are recommending people show up to catch a domestic flight, at least at some major U.S. airports with increasingly massive security lines.
sents.cn: 先是提前两小时，现在变成了三小时——至少在美国一些安检排队日益冗长的大型机场，有关部门正建议人们提早这么久赶到机场，以便乘坐国内航班。

sents.en: Americans are willing to tolerate time-consuming security procedures in return for increased safety.
sents.cn: 为了换取更高的安全性，美国人愿意忍受耗费时间的安检程序。

sents.en: The crash of Egypt Air Flight 804, which terrorists may have downed over the Mediterranean Sea, provides another tragic reminder of why.
sents.cn: 埃及航空804次航班坠毁（恐怖分子很可能将其击落于地中海上空）再次以悲剧的方式提醒人们其中的缘由。

sents.en: But demanding too much of air travelers or providing too little security in return undermines public support for the process.
sents.cn: 但对航空旅客要求过多、却只提供过少的安全保障作为回报，会削弱公众对这一程序的支持。

sents.en: And it should: Wasted time is a drag on Americans' economic and private lives, not to mention infuriating.
sents.cn: 理应如此：浪费时间不仅令人恼火，更拖累了美国人的经济生活和个人生活。

sents.en: Last year, the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) found in a secret check that undercover investigators were able to sneak weapons—both fake and real—past airport security nearly every time they tried.
sents.cn: 去年，美国运输安全管理局（TSA）在一次秘密检查中发现，便衣调查人员几乎每次都能把武器——无论是仿制的还是真的——偷偷带过机场安检。

sents.en: Enhanced security measures since then, combined with a rise in airline travel due to the improving economy and low oil prices, have resulted in long waits at major airports such as Chicago's O'Hare International.
sents.cn: 此后强化的安检措施，加之经济好转和油价走低带动航空出行增加，导致芝加哥奥黑尔国际机场等大型机场出现了长时间的等候。

sents.en: It is not yet clear how much more effective airline security has become—but the lines are obvious.
sents.cn: 航空安检究竟提升了多少效果尚不清楚——但排起的长队却是显而易见的。

sents.en: Part of the issue is that the government did not anticipate the steep increase in airline travel, so the TSA is now rushing to get new screeners on the line.
sents.cn: 问题的部分原因在于，政府没有预料到航空出行会急剧增加，因此TSA如今正手忙脚乱地调配新的安检员上岗。

sents.en: Part of the issue is that airports have only so much room for screening lanes.
sents.cn: 问题的另一部分原因在于，机场能用于设置安检通道的空间十分有限。

sents.en: Another factor may be that more people are trying to overpack their carry-on bags to avoid checked-baggage fees, though the airlines strongly dispute this.
sents.cn: 还有一个因素可能是，越来越多的人为了省下托运行李费而试图把随身行李塞得过满，尽管各航空公司对此坚决否认。

sents.en: There is one step the TSA could take that would not require remodeling airports or rushing to hire: Enroll more people in the PreCheck program.
sents.cn: TSA有一项举措既无需改造机场，也无需匆忙招人：让更多人加入“预检”（PreCheck）项目。

sents.en: PreCheck is supposed to be a win-win for travelers and the TSA.
sents.cn: “预检”本应是旅客与TSA双赢之举。

sents.en: Passengers who pass a background check are eligible to use expedited screening lanes.
sents.cn: 通过背景审查的旅客有资格使用快速安检通道。

sents.en: This allows the TSA to focus on travelers who are higher risk, saving time for everyone involved.
sents.cn: 这样TSA便可把精力集中于风险更高的旅客身上，为所有相关人员节省时间。

sents.en: The TSA wants to enroll 25 million people in PreCheck.
sents.cn: TSA希望有2500万人加入“预检”项目。

sents.en: It has not gotten anywhere close to that, and one big reason is sticker shock.
sents.cn: 然而其参与人数远未达到这一目标，一大原因便是价格令人咋舌。

sents.en: : Passengers must pay $85 every five years to process their background checks.
sents.cn: 旅客每五年必须支付85美元来办理背景审查。

sents.en: Since the beginning, this price tag has been PreCheck's fatal flaw.
sents.cn: 从一开始，这一价格就是“预检”项目的致命缺陷。

sents.en: Upcoming reforms might bring the price to a more reasonable level.
sents.cn: 即将出台的改革或许能把价格降到更合理的水平。

sents.en: But Congress should look into doing so directly, by helping to finance PreCheck enrollment or to cut costs in other ways.
sents.cn: 但国会应着手直接解决这一问题，比如出资资助“预检”报名，或通过其他途径削减成本。

sents.en: The TSA cannot continue diverting resources into underused PreCheck lanes while most of the traveling public suffers in unnecessary lines.
sents.cn: TSA不能在大多数出行公众于不必要的长队中受苦的同时，还继续把资源投入到使用率低下的“预检”通道上。

sents.en: It is long past time to make the program work.
sents.cn: 让这一项目切实发挥作用，早就是时候了。

sents.en: "The ancient Hawaiians were astronomers," wrote Queen Liliuokalani, Hawaii's last reigning monarch, in 1897.
sents.cn: “古代夏威夷人都是天文学家，”夏威夷末代在位君主利留卡拉尼女王于1897年写道。

sents.en: Star watchers were among the most esteemed members of Hawaiian society.
sents.cn: 观星者是夏威夷社会中最受尊崇的成员之一。

sents.en: Sadly, all is not well with astronomy in Hawaii today.
sents.cn: 可惜的是，如今夏威夷的天文学境况并不乐观。

sents.en: Protests have erupted over construction of the Thirty Meter Telescope (TMT), a giant observatory that promises to revolutionize humanity's view of the cosmos.
sents.cn: 围绕三十米望远镜（TMT）的建造，抗议活动爆发了；这座巨型天文台有望彻底革新人类对宇宙的认识。

sents.en: At issue is the TMT's planned location on Mauna Kea, a dormant volcano worshiped by some Hawaiians as the piko, that connects the Hawaiian Islands to the heavens.
sents.cn: 争议的焦点在于，TMT计划选址于冒纳凯阿火山——这座休眠火山被一些夏威夷人奉为“皮科”（piko），将夏威夷群岛与天界相连。

sents.en: But Mauna Kea is also home to some of the world's most powerful telescopes.
sents.cn: 但冒纳凯阿火山也是世界上一些最强大的望远镜的所在地。

sents.en: Rested in the Pacific Ocean, Mauna Kea's peak rises above the bulk of our planet's dense atmosphere, where conditions allow telescopes to obtain images of unsurpassed clarity.
sents.cn: 冒纳凯阿火山坐落于太平洋之中，其峰顶高耸于地球绝大部分稠密大气层之上，那里的条件使望远镜能够获得清晰度无与伦比的图像。

sents.en: Opposition to telescopes on Mauna Kea is nothing new.
sents.cn: 反对在冒纳凯阿火山上架设望远镜并非新鲜事。

sents.en: A small but vocal group of Hawaiians and environments have long viewed their presence as disrespect for sacred land and a painful reminder of the occupation of what was once a sovereign nation.
sents.cn: 一小群人数不多却言辞激烈的夏威夷人和环保人士，长期以来一直把望远镜的存在视为对圣地的不敬，也视为对一个昔日主权国家遭占领这段痛史的提醒。

sents.en: Some blame for the current controversy belongs to astronomers.
sents.cn: 当前这场争议，天文学家应负一部分责任。

sents.en: In their eagerness to build bigger telescopes, they forgot that science is not the only way of understanding the world.
sents.cn: 他们一心想建造更大的望远镜，却忘记了科学并非认识世界的唯一途径。

sents.en: They did not always prioritize the protection of Mauna Kea's fragile ecosystems or its holiness to the island's inhabitants.
sents.cn: 他们并不总是把保护冒纳凯阿脆弱的生态系统、以及它对岛上居民的神圣意义放在首位。

sents.en: Hawaiian culture is not a relic of the past; it is a living culture undergoing a renaissance today.
sents.cn: 夏威夷文化并非昔日的遗物；它是一种鲜活的文化，如今正经历着复兴。

sents.en: Yet science has a cultural history, too, with roots going back to the dawn of civilization.
sents.cn: 然而，科学同样有其文化历史，其根源可追溯至文明的曙光时期。

sents.en: The same curiosity to find what lies beyond the horizon that first brought early Polynesians to Hawaii's shores inspires astronomers today to explore the heavens.
sents.cn: 当年最早把波利尼西亚人带到夏威夷海岸的那种探寻地平线之外究竟有什么的好奇心，如今同样激励着天文学家去探索天界。

sents.en: Calls to disassemble all telescopes on Mauna Kea or to ban future development there ignore the reality that astronomy and Hawaiian culture both seek to answer big questions about who we are, where we come from and where we are going.
sents.cn: 那些要求拆除冒纳凯阿火山上所有望远镜、或禁止在此进行未来开发的呼声，忽视了这样一个事实：天文学与夏威夷文化都在试图回答关于我们是谁、我们从何而来、又将往何处去的重大问题。

sents.en: Perhaps that is why we explore the starry skies, as if answering a primal calling to know ourselves and our true ancestral homes.
sents.cn: 或许正因如此，我们才仰望繁星点点的夜空，仿佛是在回应一种认识自我、认识我们真正祖先家园的原始召唤。

sents.en: The astronomy community is making compromises to change its use of Mauna Kea.
sents.cn: 天文学界正在做出让步，以改变对冒纳凯阿火山的使用方式。

sents.en: The TMT site was chosen to minimize the telescope's visibility around the island and to avoid archaeological and environmental impact.
sents.cn: 选定TMT的台址，是为了尽量减小该望远镜在岛上各处的可见度，并避免对考古和环境造成影响。

sents.en: To limit the number of telescopes on Mauna Kea, old ones will be removed at the end of their lifetimes and their sites returned to a natural state.
sents.cn: 为了限制冒纳凯阿火山上望远镜的数量，旧望远镜将在使用寿命结束时被拆除，其台址也将恢复到自然状态。

sents.en: There is no reason why everyone cannot be welcomed on Mauna Kea to embrace their cultural heritage and to study the stars.
sents.cn: 没有任何理由不能欢迎每一个人来到冒纳凯阿火山，去拥抱自己的文化遗产、去研究星辰。

sents.en: Robert F. Kennedy once said that a country's GDP measures "everything except that which makes life worthwhile."
sents.cn: 罗伯特·F·肯尼迪曾说，一个国家的国内生产总值（GDP）衡量的是“除了使生活有意义的东西之外的一切”。

sents.en: With Britain voting to leave the European Union, and GDP already predicted to slow as a result, it is now a timely moment to assess what he was referring to.
sents.cn: 如今英国已投票决定脱离欧盟，GDP也因此被预计将放缓增长，此时正是评估他这番话所指为何的恰当时机。

sents.en: The question of GDP and its usefulness has annoyed policymakers for over half a century.
sents.cn: GDP及其用处的问题困扰决策者已逾半个世纪。

sents.en: Many argue that it is a flawed concept.
sents.cn: 许多人认为它是一个有缺陷的概念。

sents.en: It measures things that do not matter and misses things that do.
sents.cn: 它衡量了无关紧要的东西，却漏掉了真正重要的东西。

sents.en: By most recent measures, the UK's GDP has been the envy of the Western world, with record low unemployment and high growth figures.
sents.cn: 按多数近期指标衡量，英国的GDP一直令整个西方世界艳羡，失业率创历史新低、增长数据高企。

sents.en: If everything was going so well, then why did over 17 million people vote for Brexit, despite the warnings about what it could do to their country's economic prospects?
sents.cn: 倘若一切真的如此顺遂，那么为何有超过1700万人不顾种种关于脱欧可能危及本国经济前景的警告，仍投票支持脱欧呢？

sents.en: A recent annual study of countries and their ability to convert growth into well-being sheds some light on that question.
sents.cn: 最近一项针对各国及其将经济增长转化为民众福祉之能力的年度研究，为这一问题提供了些许启示。

sents.en: Across the 163 countries measured, the UK is one of the poorest performers in ensuring that economic growth is translated into meaningful improvements for its citizens.
sents.cn: 在所衡量的163个国家中，英国是把经济增长转化为对国民有实际意义之改善方面表现最差的国家之一。

sents.en: Rather than just focusing on GDP, over 40 different sets of criteria from health, education and civil society engagement have been measured to get a more rounded assessment of how countries are performing.
sents.cn: 该研究并未仅仅着眼于GDP，而是对健康、教育、公民社会参与等方面共40多套不同指标进行了衡量，以便更全面地评估各国的表现。

sents.en: While all of these countries face their own challenges, there are a number of consistent themes.
sents.cn: 尽管所有这些国家都面临各自的挑战，但其中存在若干一以贯之的共同主题。

sents.en: Yes, there has been a budding economic recovery since the 2008 global crash, but in key indicators in areas such as health and education, major economies have continued to decline.
sents.cn: 诚然，自2008年全球经济崩溃以来出现了初露端倪的经济复苏，但在健康、教育等领域的关键指标上，各主要经济体却持续走下坡路。

sents.en: Yet this isn't the case with all countries.
sents.cn: 然而，并非所有国家都是如此。

sents.en: Some relatively poor European countries have seen huge improvements across measures including civil society, income equality and environment.
sents.cn: 一些相对贫穷的欧洲国家在公民社会、收入平等和环境等各项指标上取得了巨大改善。

sents.en: This is a lesson that rich countries can learn: When GDP is no longer regarded as the sole measure of a country's success, the world looks very different.
sents.cn: 这是富裕国家可以汲取的一个教训：当GDP不再被视为衡量一国成功与否的唯一标准时，世界看上去便大不相同。

sents.en: So, what Kennedy was referring to was that while GDP has been the most common method for measuring the economic activity of nations, as a measure, it is no longer enough.
sents.cn: 因此，肯尼迪所指的是：尽管GDP一直是衡量各国经济活动最常用的方法，但作为一种衡量标准，它已不再够用。

sents.en: It does not include important factors such as environmental quality or education outcomes – all things that contribute to a person's sense of well-being.
sents.cn: 它并未将环境质量或教育成果等重要因素纳入其中——而这些都是有助于提升个人幸福感的东西。

sents.en: The sharp hit to growth predicted around the world and in the UK could lead to a decline in the everyday services we depend on for our well-being and for growth.
sents.cn: 据预测，全球乃至英国的经济增长将遭受重创，这可能导致我们赖以维系福祉和增长的各项日常服务出现衰退。

sents.en: But policymakers who refocus efforts on improving well-being rather than simply worrying about GDP figures could avoid the forecasted doom and may even see progress.
sents.cn: 但是，那些重新把工作重心放在改善民众福祉、而非一味担忧GDP数据上的决策者，或许能够避免所预测的厄运，甚至可能取得进步。

sents.en: In a rare unanimous ruling, the U.S Supreme Court has overturned the corruption conviction of a former Virginia governor, Robert McDonnell.
sents.cn: 美国最高法院以罕见的全体一致裁决，推翻了对弗吉尼亚州前州长罗伯特·麦克唐纳的腐败定罪。

sents.en: But it did so while holding its nose at the ethics of his conduct, which included accepting gifts such as a Rolex watch and a Ferrari Automobile from a company seeking access to government.
sents.cn: 但法院在作出这一裁决的同时，也对其行为的道德操守嗤之以鼻——他的行为包括收受一家谋求接近政府的公司所赠的劳力士手表和法拉利汽车等礼物。

sents.en: The high court's decision said the judge in Mr. McDonnell's trial failed to tell a jury that it must look only at his "official acts," or the former governor's decisions on "specific" and "unsettled" issues related to his duties.
sents.cn: 最高法院的判决指出，麦克唐纳一案的庭审法官未能告知陪审团，他们只应审视其“公务行为”，即这位前州长就与其职责相关的“具体”而“悬而未决”的问题所作出的决定。

sents.en: Merely helping a gift-giver gain access to other officials, unless done with clear intent to pressure those officials, is not corruption, the justices found.
sents.cn: 大法官们认定，仅仅帮助送礼者得以接近其他官员，除非有明确意图向那些官员施压，否则并不构成腐败。

sents.en: The court did suggest that accepting favors in return for opening doors is "distasteful" and "nasty."
sents.cn: 法院确实表示，以提供方便为交换而收受好处是“令人反感的”、“恶劣的”。

sents.en: But under anti-bribery laws, proof must be made of concrete benefits, such as approval of a contract or regulation.
sents.cn: 但根据反贿赂法，必须拿出获得具体利益的证据，例如某项合同或法规获得批准。

sents.en: Simply arranging a meeting, making a phone call, or hosting an event is not an "official act".
sents.cn: 仅仅安排一次会面、打一个电话或主办一场活动，并不属于“公务行为”。

sents.en: The court's ruling is legally sound in defining a kind of favoritism that is not criminal.
sents.cn: 法院的这一裁决在界定一种不构成犯罪的偏袒行为方面是合乎法理的。

sents.en: Elected leaders must be allowed to help supporters deal with bureaucratic problems without fear of prosecution of bribery.
sents.cn: 必须允许民选领导人帮助支持者处理官僚体制方面的问题，而不必担心因受贿而遭到起诉。

sents.en: " The basic compact underlying representative government," wrote Chief Justice John Roberts for the court," assumes that public officials will hear from their constituents and act on their concerns.
sents.cn: “代议制政府所依赖的基本约定，”首席大法官约翰·罗伯茨代表最高法院写道，“其前提是公职人员将听取选民的意见，并就他们所关切的问题采取行动。

sents.en: " But the ruling reinforces the need for citizens and their elected representatives, not the courts, to ensure equality of access to government.
sents.cn: ”但这一裁决也强化了这样一种必要性：确保民众平等地接近政府，应由公民及其民选代表来负责，而非由法院来负责。

sents.en: Officials must not be allowed to play favorites in providing information or in arranging meetings simply because an individual or group provides a campaign donation or a personal gift.
sents.cn: 绝不能允许官员仅仅因为某个人或某个团体提供了竞选捐款或个人礼物，就在提供信息或安排会面时厚此薄彼。

sents.en: This type of integrity requires well-enforced laws in government transparency, such as records of official meetings, rules on lobbying, and information about each elected leader's source of wealth.
sents.cn: 这种廉洁要求在政府透明度方面有切实得到执行的法律，例如公务会议的记录、关于游说活动的规则，以及每位民选领导人财富来源的信息。

sents.en: Favoritism in official access can fan public perceptions of corruption.
sents.cn: 在接近政府方面的偏袒，可能会助长公众对腐败的认知。

sents.en: But it is not always corruption.
sents.cn: 但它并不总是腐败。

sents.en: Rather officials must avoid double standards, or different types of access for average people and the wealthy.
sents.cn: 确切地说，官员必须避免双重标准，即避免让普通民众与富人享有不同的接近渠道。

sents.en: If connections can be bought, a basic premise of democratic society—that all are equal in treatment by government—is undermined.
sents.cn: 如果人脉关系可以用钱买到，那么民主社会的一个基本前提——人人在受到政府对待时一律平等——就会遭到破坏。

sents.en: Good governance rests on an understanding of the inherent worth of each individual.
sents.cn: 良好的治理建立在对每个人固有价值的理解之上。

sents.en: The court's ruling is a step forward in the struggle against both corruption and official favoritism.
sents.cn: 法院的这一裁决，是在反对腐败和官员偏袒这两种斗争中迈出的一步。

sents.en: Among the annoying challenges facing the middle class is one that will probably go unmentioned in the next presidential campaign: What happens when the robots come for their jobs?
sents.cn: 在中产阶级所面临的种种恼人挑战中，有一个很可能在下一届总统竞选中无人提及：当机器人来抢他们的工作时，会发生什么？

sents.en: Don't dismiss that possibility entirely.
sents.cn: 不要彻底否定这种可能性。

sents.en: About half of U.S. jobs are at high risk of being automated, according to a University of Oxford study, with the middle class disproportionately squeezed.
sents.cn: 牛津大学的一项研究表明，约有半数的美国工作岗位面临被自动化取代的高风险，而中产阶级受到的挤压尤为严重。

sents.en: Lower-income jobs like gardening or day care don't appeal to robots.
sents.cn: 园艺、日托之类的低收入工作对机器人没有吸引力。

sents.en: But many middle-class occupations-trucking, financial advice, software engineering — have aroused their interest, or soon will.
sents.cn: 但许多中产阶级职业——货运、理财顾问、软件工程——已经引起了它们的兴趣，或者很快就会。

sents.en: The rich own the robots, so they will be fine.
sents.cn: 富人拥有机器人，所以他们不会有事。

sents.en: This isn't to be alarmist.
sents.cn: 这并不是危言耸听。

sents.en: Optimists point out that technological upheaval has benefited workers in the past.
sents.cn: 乐观者指出，技术变革在过去曾使工人受益。

sents.en: The Industrial Revolution didn't go so well for Luddites whose jobs were displaced by mechanized looms, but it eventually raised living standards and created more jobs than it destroyed.
sents.cn: 工业革命对那些工作被机械织布机取代的卢德分子来说并不顺利，但它最终提高了生活水平，所创造的工作岗位多于它所摧毁的。

sents.en: Likewise, automation should eventually boost productivity, stimulate demand by driving down prices, and free workers from hard, boring work.
sents.cn: 同样，自动化最终应当会提升生产率，通过压低物价来刺激需求，并把工人从繁重、枯燥的劳动中解放出来。

sents.en: But in the medium term, middle-class workers may need a lot of help adjusting.
sents.cn: 但在中期内，中产阶级工人可能需要大量帮助来加以适应。

sents.en: The first step, as Erik Brynjolfsson and Andrew McAfee argue in The Second Machine Age, should be rethinking education and job training.
sents.cn: 正如埃里克·布林约尔松和安德鲁·麦卡菲在《第二次机器革命》中所主张的，第一步应当是重新思考教育与职业培训。

sents.en: Curriculums —from grammar school to college- should evolve to focus less on memorizing facts and more on creativity and complex communication.
sents.cn: 从小学到大学的课程都应当不断演进，减少对死记硬背事实的侧重，更多地着眼于创造力和复杂的沟通能力。

sents.en: Vocational schools should do a better job of fostering problem-solving skills and helping students work alongside robots.
sents.cn: 职业学校应当更好地培养学生解决问题的能力，并帮助他们学会与机器人协同工作。

sents.en: Online education can supplement the traditional kind.
sents.cn: 在线教育可以作为传统教育的补充。

sents.en: It could make extra training and instruction affordable.
sents.cn: 它可以让额外的培训和指导变得人人负担得起。

sents.en: Professionals trying to acquire new skills will be able to do so without going into debt.
sents.cn: 想要掌握新技能的专业人士将能够做到这一点，而无需背负债务。

sents.en: The challenge of coping with automation underlines the need for the U.S. to revive its fading business dynamism: Starting new companies must be made easier.
sents.cn: 应对自动化的挑战凸显出美国有必要重振其日渐衰退的商业活力：必须让创办新公司变得更容易。

sents.en: In previous eras of drastic technological change, entrepreneurs smoothed the transition by dreaming up ways to combine labor and machines.
sents.cn: 在以往技术剧变的时代，创业者通过构思各种将劳动力与机器结合起来的方式，使转型变得平稳。

sents.en: The best uses of 3D printers and virtual reality haven't been invented yet.
sents.cn: 3D打印机和虚拟现实的最佳用途尚未被发明出来。

sents.en: The U.S. needs the new companies that will invent them.
sents.cn: 美国需要那些将会发明这些用途的新公司。

sents.en: Finally, because automation threatens to widen the gap between capital income and labor income, taxes and the safety net will have to be rethought.
sents.cn: 最后，由于自动化有可能拉大资本收入与劳动收入之间的差距，税收和社会保障网络都将不得不重新加以考量。

sents.en: Taxes on low-wage labor need to be cut, and wage subsidies such as the earned income tax credit should be expanded: This would boost incomes, encourage work, reward companies for job creation, and reduce inequality.
sents.cn: 对低薪劳动的税收需要削减，诸如劳动所得税抵免之类的工资补贴应当扩大：这样做将提高收入、鼓励就业、奖励创造就业的企业，并减少不平等。

sents.en: Technology will improve society in ways big and small over the next few years, yet this will be little comfort to those who find their lives and careers upended by automation.
sents.cn: 在未来几年里，技术将以大大小小的方式改善社会，然而对于那些发现自己的生活和事业被自动化颠覆的人来说，这几乎算不上什么安慰。

sents.en: Destroying the machines that are coming for our jobs would be nuts.
sents.cn: 摧毁那些前来抢夺我们工作的机器将是愚蠢之举。

sents.en: But policies to help workers adapt will be indispensable.
sents.cn: 但帮助工人适应的各项政策将必不可少。

sents.en: A new survey by Harvard University finds more than two-thirds of young Americans disapprove of President Trump's use of Twitter.
sents.cn: 哈佛大学一项新的调查发现，三分之二以上的美国年轻人不赞成特朗普总统使用推特的做法。

sents.en: The implication is that Millennials prefer news from the White House to be filtered through other source, Not a president's social media platform.
sents.cn: 其含义是，千禧一代更希望来自白宫的消息经由其他渠道过滤后传出，而不是经由总统的社交媒体平台。

sents.en: Most Americans rely on social media to check daily headlines.
sents.cn: 大多数美国人依靠社交媒体来查看每日的新闻头条。

sents.en: Yet as distrust has risen toward all media, people may be starting to beef up their media literacy skills.
sents.cn: 然而，随着人们对所有媒体的不信任感上升，他们可能正开始增强自己的媒介素养。

sents.en: Such a trend is badly needed.
sents.cn: 这样一种趋势是亟需的。

sents.en: During the 2016 presidential campaign, nearly a quarter of web content shared by Twitter users in the politically critical state of Michigan was fake news, according to the University of Oxford.
sents.cn: 据牛津大学的研究，在2016年总统竞选期间，在政治上至关重要的密歇根州，推特用户分享的网络内容中近四分之一是假新闻。

sents.en: And a survey conducted for BuzzFeed News found 44 percent of Facebook users rarely or never trust news from the media giant.
sents.cn: 而一项为BuzzFeed新闻所做的调查发现，44%的脸书用户很少或从不信任这家媒体巨头的新闻。

sents.en: Young people who are digital natives are indeed becoming more skillful at separating fact from fiction in cyberspace.
sents.cn: 作为数字时代原住民的年轻人，确实在变得更善于在网络空间中辨别真伪。

sents.en: A Knight Foundation focus-group survey of young people between ages 14and24 found they use "distributed trust" to verify stories.
sents.cn: 奈特基金会针对14至24岁年轻人开展的一项焦点小组调查发现，他们运用“分散式信任”来核实报道。

sents.en: They cross-check sources and prefer news from different perspectives—especially those that are open about any bias.
sents.cn: 他们交叉核对信息来源，并偏爱来自不同视角的新闻——尤其是那些坦承自身存在偏向的新闻。

sents.en: "Many young people assume a great deal of personal responsibility for educating themselves and actively seeking out opposing viewpoints," the survey concluded.
sents.cn: 该调查总结道：“许多年轻人在自我教育、主动寻找对立观点方面承担了相当大的个人责任。”

sents.en: Such active research can have another effect.
sents.cn: 这种主动的查证还会产生另一种效果。

sents.en: A 2014 survey conducted in Australia, Britain, and the United States by the University of Wisconsin-Madison found that young people's reliance on social media led to greater political engagement.
sents.cn: 威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校2014年在澳大利亚、英国和美国开展的一项调查发现，年轻人对社交媒体的依赖促成了更高的政治参与度。

sents.en: Social media allows users to experience news events more intimately and immediately while also permitting them to re-share news as a projection of their values and interests.
sents.cn: 社交媒体让用户能够更贴近、更即时地体验新闻事件，同时也允许他们转发新闻，以此投射自己的价值观和兴趣。

sents.en: This forces users to be more conscious of their role in passing along information.
sents.cn: 这迫使用户更加意识到自己在传递信息过程中所扮演的角色。

sents.en: A survey by Barna research group found the top reason given by Americans for the fake news phenomenon is "reader error," more so than made-up stories or factual mistakes in reporting.
sents.cn: 巴纳研究集团的一项调查发现，美国人为假新闻现象给出的首要原因是“读者的错误”，其占比超过了编造的报道或报道中的事实差错。

sents.en: About a third say the problem of fake news lies in "misinterpretation or exaggeration of actual news" via social media.
sents.cn: 约有三分之一的人认为，假新闻问题在于通过社交媒体对“真实新闻的误读或夸大”。

sents.en: In other words, the choice to share news on social media may be the heart of the issue.
sents.cn: 换言之，在社交媒体上分享新闻这一选择，或许才是问题的核心所在。

sents.en: "This indicates there is a real personal responsibility in counteracting this problem," says Roxanne Stone, editor in chief at Barna Group.
sents.cn: 巴纳集团主编罗克珊·斯通说：“这表明，在抵制这一问题方面，确实存在切实的个人责任。”

sents.en: So when young people are critical of an over-tweeting president, they reveal a mental discipline in thinking skills – and in their choices on when to share on social media.
sents.cn: 因此，当年轻人批评一位过度发推的总统时，他们所展现的是一种思维能力上的心智自律——以及在何时于社交媒体上分享内容这一选择上的自律。

sents.en: Any fair-minded assessment of the dangers of the deal between Britain's National Health Service (NHS) and DeepMind must start by acknowledging that both sides mean well.
sents.cn: 对英国国民医疗服务体系（NHS）与DeepMind之间这笔合作所蕴含危险的任何公正评估，都必须首先承认双方的出发点都是善意的。

sents.en: DeepMind is one of the leading artificial intelligence (AI) companies in the world.
sents.cn: DeepMind是全球领先的人工智能（AI）公司之一。

sents.en: The potential of this work applied to healthcare is very great, but it could also lead to further concentration of power in the tech giants.
sents.cn: 这项工作应用于医疗保健领域的潜力极为巨大，但它也可能导致权力进一步向科技巨头集中。

sents.en: It Is against that background that the information commissioner, Elizabeth Denham, has issued her damning verdict against the Royal Free hospital trust under the NHS, which handed over to DeepMind the records of 1.6 million patients In 2015 on the basis of a vague agreement which took far too little account of the patients' rights and their expectations of privacy.
sents.cn: 正是在这一背景之下，信息专员伊丽莎白·德纳姆对隶属于NHS的皇家自由医院信托基金做出了措辞严厉的裁定：该信托基金于2015年依据一份含糊的协议，将160万名患者的记录移交给了DeepMind，而这份协议远远没有充分顾及患者的权利及其对隐私的期望。

sents.en: DeepMind has almost apologized.
sents.cn: DeepMind几乎已经道歉。

sents.en: The NHS trust has mended its ways.
sents.cn: 这家NHS信托基金已经改正了做法。

sents.en: Further arrangements- and there may be many-between the NHS and DeepMind will be carefully scrutinised to ensure that all necessary permissions have been asked of patients and all unnecessary data has been cleaned.
sents.cn: NHS与DeepMind之间今后的各项安排——而且这类安排可能会有许多——都将受到仔细审查，以确保已向患者征得一切必要的许可，并已清除所有不必要的数据。

sents.en: There are lessons about informed patient consent to learn.
sents.cn: 其中有关于患者知情同意的教训值得汲取。

sents.en: But privacy is not the only angle in this case and not even the most important.
sents.cn: 但隐私并非本案中唯一的视角，甚至算不上最重要的视角。

sents.en: Ms Denham chose to concentrate the blame on the NHS trust, since under existing law it "controlled" the data and DeepMind merely "processed" it.
sents.cn: 德纳姆女士选择把责任集中追究于这家NHS信托基金，因为依据现行法律，是它“控制”了数据，而DeepMind只是“处理”了数据。

sents.en: But this distinction misses the point that it is processing and aggregation, not the mere possession of bits, that gives the data value.
sents.cn: 但这种区分忽略了一点：真正赋予数据价值的，是对数据的处理与汇聚，而非仅仅对那些比特位的占有。

sents.en: The great question is who should benefit from the analysis of all the data that our lives now generate.
sents.cn: 最大的问题在于，谁应当从对我们如今生活所产生的全部数据的分析中获益。

sents.en: Privacy law builds on the concept of damage to an individual from identifiable knowledge about them.
sents.cn: 隐私法建立在这样一个概念之上：可识别个人身份的信息会对个人造成损害。

sents.en: That misses the way the surveillance economy works.
sents.cn: 这忽略了监控经济的运作方式。

sents.en: The data of an individual there gains its value only when it is compared with the data of countless millions more.
sents.cn: 在这种经济中，个人的数据只有在与另外亿万人的数据进行比对时，才会显现出价值。

sents.en: The use of privacy law to curb the tech giants in this instance feels slightly maladapted.
sents.cn: 在本例中动用隐私法来约束科技巨头，让人感觉略有些不对路。

sents.en: This practice does not address the real worry.
sents.cn: 这种做法并未触及真正令人担忧之处。

sents.en: It is not enough to say that the algorithms DeepMind develops will benefit patients and save lives.
sents.cn: 仅仅说DeepMind所开发的算法将造福患者、挽救生命，是远远不够的。

sents.en: What matters is that they will belong to a private monopoly which developed them using public resources.
sents.cn: 关键在于，这些算法将归属于一家利用公共资源将其开发出来的私营垄断企业。

sents.en: If software promises to save lives on the scale that drugs now can, big data may be expected to behave as big pharma has done.
sents.cn: 如果软件有望以如今药物所能达到的规模去挽救生命，那么人们就可以预料，大数据将会像大型制药企业那样行事。

sents.en: We are still at the beginning of this revolution and small choices now may turn out to have gigantic consequences later.
sents.cn: 我们仍处于这场革命的开端，如今的一些细小选择，日后或许会带来极其巨大的后果。

sents.en: A long struggle will be needed to avoid a future of digital feudalism.
sents.cn: 要避免一个数字封建主义的未来，将需要一场长期的斗争。

sents.en: Ms Denham's report is a welcome start.
sents.cn: 德纳姆女士的报告是一个值得欢迎的开端。

sents.en: The U.S. Postal Service (USPS) continues to bleed red ink.
sents.cn: 美国邮政总局（USPS）持续亏损不止。

sents.en: It reported a net loss of $5.6 billion for fiscal 2016, the 10th straight year its expenses have exceeded revenue.
sents.cn: 它报告2016财年净亏损56亿美元，这是其支出超过收入的连续第十个年头。

sents.en: Meanwhile, it has more than $120 billion in unfunded liabilities, mostly for employee health and retirement costs.
sents.cn: 与此同时，它还背负着逾1200亿美元没有资金来源的债务，其中大部分用于员工的医疗和退休费用。

sents.en: Fundamentally, the USPS is in a historic squeeze between technological change that has permanently decreased demand for its bread-and-butter product, first-class mail, and a regulatory structure that denies management the flexibility to adjust its operations to the new reality And interest groups ranging from postal unions to greeting-card makers exert self-interested pressure on the USPS's ultimate overseer-Congress-insisting that whatever else happens to the Postal Service, aspects of the status quo they depend on get protected.
sents.cn: 从根本上说，美国邮政总局正陷入一场历史性的双重挤压之中：一方面，技术变革永久性地减少了对其看家产品——平信业务的需求；另一方面，一种监管体制使管理层无法灵活地调整运营以适应新的现实。而从邮政工会到贺卡制造商等形形色色的利益集团，都向美国邮政总局的最终监管者——国会——施加出于自身利益的压力，坚持要求无论邮政服务遭遇什么变故，他们所依赖的现状的某些方面都必须得到保护。

sents.en: This is why repeated attempts at reform legislation have failed in recent years, leaving the Postal Service unable to pay its bills except by deferring vital modernization.
sents.cn: 这正是近年来一再尝试的改革立法均告失败的原因，结果使邮政服务除了推迟至关重要的现代化改造之外，无力支付其各项账单。

sents.en: Now comes word that everyone involved---Democrats, Republicans, the Postal Service, the unions and the system's heaviest users—has finally agreed on a plan to fix the system.
sents.cn: 如今传来消息称，所有相关方——民主党人、共和党人、邮政服务部门、各工会以及该系统最大的用户——终于就一项整顿该系统的方案达成了一致。

sents.en: Legislation is moving through the House that would save USPS an estimated $28.6 billion over five years, which could help pay for new vehicles, among other survival measures.
sents.cn: 一项立法正在众议院推进，据估计可在五年内为美国邮政总局节省286亿美元，这笔钱除用于其他求生举措外，还有助于支付购置新车辆的费用。

sents.en: Most of the money would come from a penny-per-letter permanent rate increase and from shifting postal retirees into Medicare.
sents.cn: 这笔钱大部分将来自每封信件永久上调一美分的资费，以及把邮政退休人员转入联邦医疗保险（Medicare）。

sents.en: The latter step would largely offset the financial burden of annually pre-funding retiree health care, thus addressing a long-standing complaint by the USPS and its union.
sents.cn: 后一项举措将在很大程度上抵消每年为退休人员医疗保健预先拨付资金所带来的财务负担，从而解决美国邮政总局及其工会长期以来的一项不满。

sents.en: If it clears the House, this measure would still have to get through the Senate – where someone is bound to point out that it amounts to the bare, bare minimum necessary to keep the Postal Service afloat, not comprehensive reform.
sents.cn: 这项法案即便在众议院获得通过，仍须闯过参议院这一关——届时必定有人会指出，它充其量只是维持邮政服务勉强运转所必需的最最低限度的措施，而非全面改革。

sents.en: There's no change to collective bargaining at the USPS, a major omission considering that personnel accounts for 80 percent of the agency's costs.
sents.cn: 美国邮政总局的集体谈判制度毫无变动，而鉴于人员开支占该机构成本的80%，这是一个重大的疏漏。

sents.en: Also missing is any discussion of eliminating Saturday letter delivery.
sents.cn: 同样缺失的，是任何关于取消周六信件投递的讨论。

sents.en: That common-sense change enjoys wide public support and would save the USPS $2 billion per year.
sents.cn: 这一合乎常理的变革赢得了公众的广泛支持，并将每年为美国邮政总局节省20亿美元。

sents.en: But postal special-interest groups seem to have killed it, at least in the House.
sents.cn: 但邮政方面的特殊利益集团似乎已将其扼杀，至少在众议院是如此。

sents.en: The emerging consensus around the bill is a sign that legislators are getting frightened about a politically embarrassing short-term collapse at the USPS.
sents.cn: 围绕该法案正在形成的共识表明，立法者们对美国邮政总局可能出现的、在政治上令人难堪的短期崩溃感到担忧。

sents.en: It is not, however, a sign that they're getting serious about transforming the postal system for the 21st century.
sents.cn: 然而，这并不表明他们在认真对待如何为21世纪改造邮政系统这一问题。

sents.en: Financial regulators in Britain have imposed a rather unusual rule on the bosses of big banks.
sents.cn: 英国的金融监管机构对各大银行的高管施加了一项颇为不同寻常的规定。

sents.en: Starting next year, any guaranteed bonus of top executives could be delayed 10 years if their banks are under investigation for wrongdoing.
sents.cn: 从明年起，如果银行因不当行为而正在接受调查，其高级管理人员的任何已承诺发放的奖金都可能被推迟十年发放。

sents.en: The main purpose of this "clawback" rule is to hold bankers accountable for harmful risk-taking and to restore public trust in financial institution.
sents.cn: 这项“追回”规定的主要目的，是让银行家为其有害的冒险行为承担责任，并重建公众对金融机构的信任。

sents.en: Yet officials also hope for a much larger benefit: more long-term decision-making, not only by banks but by all corporations, to build a stronger economy for future generations.
sents.cn: 然而，官员们还期望获得一项大得多的益处：促使人们做出更多着眼长远的决策——不仅银行如此，所有企业都应如此——从而为子孙后代打造一个更为强健的经济。

sents.en: "Short-termism" or the desire for quick profits, has worsened in publicly traded companies, says the Bank of England's top economist, Andrew Haldane.
sents.cn: 英格兰银行首席经济学家安德鲁·霍尔丹表示，“短视行为”，即对快速获利的渴求，在上市公司中已愈演愈烈。

sents.en: He quotes a giant of classical economics, Alfred Marshall, in describing this financial impatience as acting like "children who pick the plums out of their pudding to eat them at once" rather than putting them aside to be eaten last.
sents.cn: 他援引古典经济学巨匠阿尔弗雷德·马歇尔的话，把这种金融上的急功近利描述为：其行事方式如同“把布丁里的梅子挑出来立刻吃掉”的孩子，而不是把梅子留到最后再吃。

sents.en: The average time for holding a stock in both the United States and Britain, he notes, has dropped from seven years to seven months in recent decades.
sents.cn: 他指出，近几十年来，美国和英国持有一只股票的平均时间已从七年缩短到七个月。

sents.en: Transient investors, who demand high quarterly profits from companies, can hinder a firm's efforts to invest in long-term research or to build up customer loyalty.
sents.cn: 那些向公司索要高额季度利润的短线投资者，会阻碍企业投资于长期研究或培养顾客忠诚度的努力。

sents.en: This has been dubbed "quarterly capitalism"
sents.cn: 这种现象被称为“季度资本主义”。

sents.en: In addition, new digital technologies have allowed more rapid trading of equities, quicker use of information, and thus shorter attention spans in financial markets.
sents.cn: 此外，新的数字技术使股票交易更为快捷，信息的运用更为迅速，因而也使金融市场上人们的注意力持续时间更短。

sents.en: "There seems to be a predominance of short-term thinking at the expense of long-term investing," said Commissioner Daniel Gallagher of the US Securities and Exchange Commission in a speech this week.
sents.cn: 美国证券交易委员会委员丹尼尔·加拉格尔本周在一次演讲中说：“短期思维似乎占了上风，而长期投资则为此付出了代价。”

sents.en: In the US, the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 has pushed most public companies to defer performance bonuses for senior executives by about a year, slightly helping reduce "short-termism."
sents.cn: 在美国，2002年的《萨班斯—奥克斯利法案》已促使大多数上市公司将高级管理人员的绩效奖金推迟约一年发放，这略微有助于减少“短视行为”。

sents.en: In its latest survey of CEO pay, The Wall Street Journal finds that "a substantial part " of executive pay is now tied to performance.
sents.cn: 《华尔街日报》在其最新一期关于首席执行官薪酬的调查中发现，如今高管薪酬中“相当大的一部分”已与业绩挂钩。

sents.en: Much more could be done to encourage "long-termism," such as changes in the tax code and quicker disclosure of stock acquisitions.
sents.cn: 为鼓励“长远主义”，还有很多事情可做，比如修改税法以及更快地披露股票收购信息。

sents.en: In France, shareholders who hold onto a company investment for at least two years can sometimes earn more voting rights in a company.
sents.cn: 在法国，持有某公司投资至少两年的股东，有时可以在该公司获得更多的投票权。

sents.en: Within companies, the right compensation design can provide incentives for executives to think beyond their own time at the company and on behalf of all stakeholders.
sents.cn: 在企业内部，恰当的薪酬设计能够激励管理人员超越自己在公司任职的那段时间来思考问题，并代表所有利益相关者的利益。

sents.en: Britain's new rule is a reminder to bankers that society has an interest in their performance, not just for the short term but for the long term.
sents.cn: 英国的这项新规提醒银行家：社会对他们的业绩抱有关切，这种关切不仅着眼于短期，更着眼于长远。

sents.en: Grade inflation—the gradual increase in average GPAs (grade-point averages) over the past few decades—is often considered a product of a consumer era in higher education, in which students are treated like customers to be pleased.
sents.cn: 分数膨胀——即过去几十年里平均绩点（GPA）的逐渐攀升——常被视为高等教育消费时代的产物，在这个时代里，学生被当作需要取悦的顾客。

sents.en: But another, related force—a policy often buried deep in course catalogs called "grade forgiveness"— is helping raise GPAs.
sents.cn: 但另一种与之相关的力量——一项常常深埋于课程目录之中、名为“成绩豁免”的政策——也在推高绩点。

sents.en: Grade forgiveness allows students to retake a course in which they received a low grade, and the most recent grade or the highest grade is the only one that counts in calculating a student's overall GPA.
sents.cn: 成绩豁免允许学生重修某门得了低分的课程，在计算学生总绩点时，只有最近一次的成绩或最高的成绩才被计入。

sents.en: The use of this little-known practice has accelerated in recent years, as colleges continue to do their utmost to keep students in school (and paying tuition) and improve their graduation rates.
sents.cn: 近年来，随着高校继续竭尽全力把学生留在校内（并继续缴纳学费）、提高毕业率，这种鲜为人知的做法日益盛行。

sents.en: When this practice first started decades ago, it was usually limited to freshmen, to give them a second chance to take a class in their first year if they struggled in their transition to college-level courses.
sents.cn: 几十年前这种做法刚兴起时，通常仅限于大一新生，目的是在他们难以适应大学水平课程时，给他们一次在第一学年重修某门课的机会。

sents.en: But now most colleges, save for many selective campuses, allow all undergraduates, and even graduate students, to get their low grades forgiven.
sents.cn: 但如今，除许多名校外，大多数高校都允许所有本科生、乃至研究生豁免自己的低分成绩。

sents.en: College officials tend to emphasize that the goal of grade forgiveness is less about the grade itself and more about encouraging students to retake courses critical to their degree program and graduation without incurring a big penalty.
sents.cn: 高校官员往往强调，成绩豁免的目的不在于分数本身，而更在于鼓励学生重修那些对其学位课程和顺利毕业至关重要的课程，同时又不至于付出沉重代价。

sents.en: "Untimely," said Jack Miner, Ohio State University's registrar, "we see students achieve more success because they retake a course and do better in subsequent contents or master the content that allows them to graduate on time."
sents.cn: 俄亥俄州立大学教务长杰克·迈纳说：“我们时常看到学生取得了更大的成功，因为他们重修了某门课程，在后续内容中表现更佳，或者掌握了使他们得以按时毕业的知识。”

sents.en: That said, there is a way in which grade forgiveness satisfies colleges' own needs as well.
sents.cn: 话虽如此，成绩豁免也以某种方式满足着高校自身的需求。

sents.en: For public institutions, state funds are sometimes tied partly to their success on metrics such as graduation rates and student retention—so better grades can, by boosting figures like those, mean more money.
sents.cn: 对于公立院校而言，州政府拨款有时与其在毕业率、学生留存率等指标上的表现部分挂钩——因此，更高的分数通过提升这类数据，便可能意味着更多的经费。

sents.en: And anything that raises GPAs will likely make students—who, at the end of the day, are paying the bill—feel they've gotten a better value for their tuition dollars, which is another big concern for colleges.
sents.cn: 而且，任何能提高绩点的做法都很可能让学生——归根结底是他们在埋单——觉得自己所缴的学费物有所值，而这正是高校另一桩颇为关心的事。

sents.en: Indeed, grade forgiveness is just another way that universities are responding to consumers' expectations for higher education.
sents.cn: 的确，成绩豁免只是大学顺应消费者对高等教育期望的又一种方式。

sents.en: Since students and parents expect a college degree to lead to a job, it is in the best interest of a school to turn out graduates who are as qualified as possible—or at least appear to be.
sents.cn: 既然学生和家长都指望大学文凭能带来一份工作，那么尽可能培养出合格的毕业生——或者至少看上去合格——便最符合学校的利益。

sents.en: On this, students' and colleges' incentives seem to be aligned.
sents.cn: 在这一点上，学生与高校的动机似乎是一致的。

sents.en: This year marks exactly two centuries since the publication of "Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus," by Mary Shelley.
sents.cn: 今年恰逢玛丽·雪莱的《弗兰肯斯坦：或现代普罗米修斯》出版整整两百周年。

sents.en: Even before the invention of the electric light bulb, the author produced a remarkable work of speculative fiction that would foreshadow many ethical questions to be raised by technologies yet to come.
sents.cn: 甚至早在电灯泡发明之前，这位作者就创作出了一部非凡的推想小说，预示了日后种种尚未问世的技术将会引发的诸多伦理问题。

sents.en: Today the rapid growth of artificial intelligence (AI) raises fundamental questions: "What is intelligence, identity, or consciousness? What makes humans humans?"
sents.cn: 如今，人工智能（AI）的迅猛发展引出了一些根本性的问题：“何为智能、身份认同或意识？是什么使人之为人？”

sents.en: What is being called artificial general intelligence, machines that would imitate the way humans think, continues to evade scientists.
sents.cn: 被称为通用人工智能的东西，即能够模仿人类思维方式的机器，依然让科学家们难以企及。

sents.en: Yet humans remain fascinated by the idea of robots that would look, move, and respond like humans, similar to those recently depicted on popular sci-fi TV series such as "West world" and "Humans".
sents.cn: 然而，人类仍然为这样一种设想所着迷：机器人在外形、动作和反应上都像人一样，正如近来在《西部世界》《真实的人类》等热门科幻电视剧中所描绘的那样。

sents.en: Just how people think is still far too complex to be understood, let alone reproduced, says David Eagleman, a Stanford University neuroscientist.
sents.cn: 斯坦福大学神经科学家戴维·伊格曼说，人究竟是如何思考的，至今仍过于复杂而难以理解，更遑论加以复制。

sents.en: "We are just in a situation where there are no good theories explaining what consciousness actually is and how you could ever build a machine to get there."
sents.cn: “我们正处于这样一种境地：没有任何好的理论能够解释意识究竟是什么，以及人怎样才可能造出一台机器达到那种境界。”

sents.en: But that doesn't mean crucial ethical issues involving AI aren't at hand.
sents.cn: 但这并不意味着事关人工智能的重大伦理问题尚未临近。

sents.en: The coming use of autonomous vehicles, for example, poses thorny ethical questions.
sents.cn: 例如，自动驾驶汽车即将投入使用，便带来了棘手的伦理问题。

sents.en: Human drivers sometimes must make split-second decisions.
sents.cn: 人类驾驶员有时必须在瞬间做出决断。

sents.en: Their reactions may be a complex combination of instant reflexes, input from past driving experiences, and what their eyes and ears tell them in that moment.
sents.cn: 他们的反应也许是即时本能反应、以往驾驶经验所提供的信息以及当下耳闻目睹之物的复杂综合。

sents.en: AI "vision" today is not nearly as sophisticated as that of humans.
sents.cn: 如今人工智能的“视觉”远不及人类的视觉那般精密复杂。

sents.en: And to anticipate every imaginable driving situation is a difficult programming problem.
sents.cn: 而要预先设想到每一种可以想象到的驾驶情形，则是一个棘手的编程难题。

sents.en: Whenever decisions are based on masses of data, "you quickly get into a lot of ethical questions," notes Tan Kiat How, chief executive of a Singapore-based agency that is helping the government develop a voluntary code for the ethical use of AI.
sents.cn: 一旦决策建立在海量数据之上，“你很快就会陷入一大堆伦理问题”，陈杰豪指出；他是新加坡一家机构的首席执行官，该机构正协助政府制定一套人工智能合乎伦理使用的自愿性准则。

sents.en: Along with Singapore, other governments and mega-corporations are beginning to establish their own guidelines.
sents.cn: 与新加坡一样，其他一些政府和巨型企业也开始制定各自的准则。

sents.en: Britain is setting up a data ethics center.
sents.cn: 英国正在筹建一个数据伦理中心。

sents.en: India released its AI ethics strategy this spring.
sents.cn: 印度则在今年春天发布了其人工智能伦理战略。

sents.en: On June 7 Google pledged not to "design or deploy AI" that would cause "overall harm," or to develop AI-directed weapons or use AI for surveillance that would violate international norms.
sents.cn: 6月7日，谷歌承诺不会“设计或部署”会造成“整体危害”的人工智能，也不会研发由人工智能操控的武器，或将人工智能用于违反国际准则的监控。

sents.en: It also pledged not to deploy AI whose use would violate international laws or human rights.
sents.cn: 它还承诺不会部署其使用会违反国际法或人权的人工智能。

sents.en: While the statement is vague, it represents one starting point.
sents.cn: 尽管这份声明含糊其辞，它却代表了一个起点。

sents.en: So does the idea that decisions made by AI systems should be explainable, transparent, and fair.
sents.cn: 人工智能系统所做的决策应当可解释、透明且公正这一理念，同样是一个起点。

sents.en: To put it another way: How can we make sure that the thinking of intelligent machines reflects humanity's highest values?
sents.cn: 换言之：我们怎样才能确保智能机器的思维体现出人类的最高价值观？

sents.en: Only then will they be useful servants and not Frankenstein's out-of-control monster.
sents.cn: 唯有如此，它们才会成为有用的仆人，而不是弗兰肯斯坦那失控的怪物。

sents.en: States will be able to force more people to pay sales tax when they make online purchases under a Supreme Court decision Thursday that will leave shoppers with lighter wallets but is a big financial win for states.
sents.cn: 根据最高法院周四作出的一项裁决，各州将能够强制更多的人在网上购物时缴纳销售税；这一裁决会让购物者的钱包更瘪，但对各州而言却是一场重大的财政胜利。

sents.en: The Supreme Court's opinion Thursday overruled a pair of decades-old decisions that states said cost them billions of dollars in lost revenue annually.
sents.cn: 最高法院周四的意见推翻了两项已有数十年之久的判决；各州称，这两项判决每年使它们损失数十亿美元的收入。

sents.en: The decisions made it more difficult for states to collect sales tax on certain online purchases.
sents.cn: 这两项判决使各州更难对某些网上购物征收销售税。

sents.en: The cases the court overturned said that if a business was shipping a customer's purchase to a state where the business didn't have a physical presence such as a warehouse or office, the business didn't have to collect sales tax for the state.
sents.cn: 被法院推翻的这些案件曾裁定：如果一家企业把顾客所购商品运往某个州，而该企业在该州并无仓库、办事处之类的实体存在，那么这家企业就无需为该州代收销售税。

sents.en: Customers were generally responsible for paying the sales tax to the state themselves if they weren't charged it, but most didn't realize they owed it and few paid.
sents.cn: 一般而言，如果顾客未被征收销售税，便应由其自行向所在州缴纳，但大多数人并未意识到自己负有此项纳税义务，真正缴纳的人寥寥无几。

sents.en: Justice Anthony Kennedy wrote that the previous decisions were flawed.
sents.cn: 大法官安东尼·肯尼迪写道，先前的那些判决存在缺陷。

sents.en: "Each year the physical presence rule becomes further removed from economic reality and results in significant revenue losses to the States," he wrote in an opinion joined by four other justices.
sents.cn: 他在一份得到另外四位大法官附议的意见书中写道：“实体存在规则逐年与经济现实越发脱节，并导致各州蒙受巨大的收入损失。”

sents.en: Kennedy wrote that the rule "limited state' ability to seek long-term prosperity and has prevented market participants from competing on an even playing field."
sents.cn: 肯尼迪写道，该规则“限制了各州谋求长期繁荣的能力，并妨碍了市场参与者在公平的环境中展开竞争”。

sents.en: The ruling is a victory for big chains with a presence in many states, since they usually collect sales tax on online purchases already.
sents.cn: 这一裁决对那些在许多州都设有门店的大型连锁企业来说是一场胜利，因为它们通常本就对网上购物代收销售税。

sents.en: Now, rivals will be charging sales tax where they hadn't before.
sents.cn: 如今，它们的竞争对手也将在以往不征税的地方征收销售税。

sents.en: Big chains have been collecting sales tax nationwide because they typically have physical stores in whatever state a purchase is being shipped to.
sents.cn: 大型连锁企业一直在全国范围内代收销售税，因为无论商品被运往哪个州，它们在该州通常都设有实体门店。

sents.en: Amazon.com, with its network of warehouses, also collects sales tax in every state that charges it, though third-party sellers who use the site don't have to.
sents.cn: 亚马逊公司凭借其仓库网络，也在每一个征收销售税的州代收销售税，不过使用该网站的第三方卖家则无需代收。

sents.en: Until now, many sellers that have a physical presence in only a single state or a few states have been able to avoid charging sales taxes when they ship to addresses outside those states.
sents.cn: 迄今为止，许多仅在单一州或少数几个州设有实体存在的卖家，在向这些州以外的地址发货时一直得以免于征收销售税。

sents.en: Sellers that use eBay and Etsy, which provide platforms for smaller sellers, also haven't been collecting sales tax nationwide.
sents.cn: 使用为小卖家提供平台的eBay和Etsy的卖家，同样一直未在全国范围内代收销售税。

sents.en: Under the ruling Thursday, states can pass laws requiring out-of-state sellers to collect the state's sales tax from customers and send it to the state.
sents.cn: 根据周四的这项裁决，各州可以通过立法，要求外州卖家向顾客代收本州的销售税并上缴本州。

sents.en: Retail trade groups praised the ruling, saying it levels the playing field for local and online businesses.
sents.cn: 各零售业行业团体对这一裁决表示赞许，称它为本地企业和网络企业营造了公平的竞争环境。

sents.en: The losers, said retail analyst Neil Saunders, are online-only retailers, especially smaller ones.
sents.cn: 零售业分析师尼尔·桑德斯说，输家是那些纯网络零售商，尤其是其中规模较小的那些。

sents.en: Those retailers may face headaches complying with various state sales tax laws.
sents.cn: 这些零售商在遵守各州五花八门的销售税法规时，可能会遇到不少麻烦。

sents.en: The Small Business & Entrepreneurship Council advocacy group said in a statement, "Small businesses and internet entrepreneurs are not well served at all by this decision."
sents.cn: 小企业与创业理事会这一倡导团体在一份声明中表示：“这项裁决对小企业和互联网创业者根本毫无益处。”
